29. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "head -100 /workspace/.github/workflows/unstable.yml",
  "description": "View unstable workflow"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "name: unstable\n\non:\n  push:\n    branches:\n      - main\n    tags:\n      - ciflow/unstable/*\n  workflow_dispatch:\n\nconcurrency:\n  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\n  cancel-in-progress: true\n\npermissions:\n  id-token: write\n  contents: read\n\njobs:\n  # There must be at least one job here to satisfy GitHub action workflow syntax\n  introduction:\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    continue-on-error: true\n    steps:\n      - name: Introduce PyTorch unstable workflow\n        run: |\n          echo \"PyTorch unstable workflow is used to host experimental or flaky jobs\"\n          echo \" that needs to be run for every commit, but doesn't block PR merging\"\n          echo \" as part of the stable pull or trunk workflows.\"\n          echo\n          echo \"In addition, a new label called ciflow/unstable can be attached to the\"\n          echo \" PR to trigger this workflow. That can be done either manually or\"\n          echo \" automatically using PyTorch auto-label bot.\"\n          echo\n          echo \"Once the jobs are deemed stable enough (% red signal < 5% and TTS < 3h),\"\n          echo \" they can graduate and move back to pull or trunk.\"\n\n  target-determination:\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    name: before-test\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/target_determination.yml\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n\n  get-label-type:\n    name: get-label-type\n    uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n    if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    with:\n      triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\n      issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\n      curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\n      curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}"
}
30. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "head -100 /workspace/.github/workflows/periodic.yml",
  "description": "View periodic workflow"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "name: periodic\n\non:\n  schedule:\n    # We have several schedules so jobs can check github.event.schedule to activate only for a fraction of the runs.\n    # Also run less frequently on weekends.\n    - cron: 45 0,8,16 * * 1-5\n    - cron: 45 4 * * 0,6\n    - cron: 45 4,12,20 * * 1-5\n    - cron: 45 12 * * 0,6\n    - cron: 29 8 * * *  # about 1:29am PDT, for mem leak check and rerun disabled tests\n  push:\n    tags:\n      - ciflow/periodic/*\n    branches:\n      - release/*\n  workflow_dispatch:\n\nconcurrency:\n  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}-${{ github.event.schedule }}\n  cancel-in-progress: true\n\npermissions:\n  id-token: write\n  contents: read\n\njobs:\n  llm-td:\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    name: before-test\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/llm_td_retrieval.yml\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n\n  target-determination:\n    name: before-test\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/target_determination.yml\n    needs: llm-td\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n\n  get-label-type:\n    name: get-label-type\n    uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n    if: (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    with:\n      triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\n      issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\n      curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\n      curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\n\n  linux-jammy-cuda12_4-py3_10-gcc11-build:\n    name: linux-jammy-cuda12.4-py3.10-gcc11\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.4-py3.10-gcc11\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.4-cudnn9-py3-gcc11\n      cuda-arch-list: 7.5\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"legacy_nvidia_driver\", shard: 1, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g4dn.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu\" },\n          { config: \"legacy_nvidia_driver\", shard: 2, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g4dn.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu\" },\n          { config: \"legacy_nvidia_driver\", shard: 3, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g4dn.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu\" },\n          { config: \"legacy_nvidia_driver\", shard: 4, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g4dn.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu\" },\n          { config: \"legacy_nvidia_driver\", shard: 5, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g4dn.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu\" },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-cuda12_4-py3_10-gcc11-test:\n    name: linux-jammy-cuda12.4-py3.10-gcc11\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\n    needs:\n      - linux-jammy-cuda12_4-py3_10-gcc11-build\n      - target-determination\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_4-py3_10-gcc11-build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_4-py3_10-gcc11-build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_4-py3_10-gcc11-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-build:\n    name: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11\n      cuda-arch-list: 8.6\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"nogpu_AVX512\", shard: 1, num_shards: 3, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"nogpu_AVX512\", shard: 2, num_shards: 3, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"nogpu_AVX512\", shard: 3, num_shards: 3, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"nogpu_NO_AVX2\", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"nogpu_NO_AVX2\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },"
}

(github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    name: linux-jammy-rocm-py3.10\n    uses: needs.linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n    secrets: inherit"

40. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "head -100 /workspace/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml",
  "description": "View runner determinator workflow"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "name: Check whether the workflow owner can use ARC runners\n\non:\n  workflow_call:\n    inputs:\n      check_experiments:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: |\n          List of experiments for this workflow. If not defined, all default experiments are included.\n      opt_out_experiments:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: Comma-separated list of experiments this workflow will opt-out of.\n      triggering_actor:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: The triggering_actor for the workflow. Use github.triggering_actor\n      issue_owner:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: The owner of the issue. Use github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login\n      curr_branch:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Current branch or tag.\n      curr_ref_type:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: branch\n        description: The value of \"github.ref_type\", \"branch\" or \"tag\"\n      issue_number:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"5132\"\n        description: |\n          Fetch's GitHub Issue from pytorch/test-infra\n          Example: https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/5132\n\n    outputs:\n      label-type:\n        description: Type of runners to use\n        value: ${{ jobs.runner-determinator.outputs.label-type }}\n\njobs:\n  runner-determinator:\n    # Don't run on forked repos\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    outputs:\n      label-type: ${{ steps.set-condition.outputs.label-type }}\n    env:\n      GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n      ISSUE_NUMBER: ${{ inputs.issue_number }}\n      TRIGGERING_ACTOR: ${{ inputs.triggering_actor }}\n      ISSUE_OWNER: ${{ inputs.issue_owner }}\n      CHECK_EXPERIMENTS: ${{ inputs.check_experiments }}\n      OPT_OUT_EXPERIMENTS: ${{ inputs.opt_out_experiments }}\n      PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n    steps:\n      # - name: Checkout PyTorch\n      #   uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n      #   with:\n      #     fetch-depth: 1\n      #     submodules: true\n\n      # TODO: Remove the hardcoded step below\n      # Hardcoding below is temporary for testing ALI runners\n      # This file below should match the script found in .github/scripts/runner_determinator.py\n      - name: Hardcode runner-determinator script\n        id: hardcode-script\n        run: |\n          cat <<EOF > runner_determinator.py\n          # flake8: noqa: G004\n\n          # Note: Copies of this script in runner_determinator.py and _runner-determinator.yml\n          #       must be kept in sync. You can do it easily by running the following command:\n          #           python .github/scripts/update_runner_determinator.py\n\n          \"\"\"\n          This runner determinator is used to determine which set of runners to run a\n          GitHub job on. It uses the first comment of a GitHub issue (by default\n          https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/5132) to define the configuration\n          of which runners should be used to run which job.\n\n          The configuration has two parts, the settings and a list of opted-in users,\n          separated by a line containing \"---\".  If the line is not present, the\n          settings are considered to be empty with only the second part, the user\n          list, defined.\n\n          The first part is a YAML block that defines the rollout settings. This can be\n          used to define any settings that are needed to determine which runners to use.\n          It's fields are defined by the RolloutSettings class below.\n\n          The second part is a list of users who are explicitly opted in to the LF fleet.\n          The user list is also a comma separated list of additional features or\n          experiments which the user could be opted in to.\n\n          The user list has the following rules:"
}

46. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/build-almalinux-images.yml"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "name: Build almalinux docker images\n\non:\n  workflow_dispatch:\n  push:\n    branches:\n      - main\n      - release/*\n    tags:\n      # NOTE: Binary build pipelines should only get triggered on release candidate or nightly builds\n      # Release candidate tags look like: v1.11.0-rc1\n      - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+-rc[0-9]+\n    paths:\n      - .ci/docker/**\n      - .github/workflows/build-almalinux-images.yml\n      - .github/actions/binary-docker-build/**\n  pull_request:\n    paths:\n      - .ci/docker/**\n      - .github/workflows/build-almalinux-images.yml\n      - .github/actions/binary-docker-build/**\n\nenv:\n  DOCKER_REGISTRY: \"docker.io\"\n  DOCKER_BUILDKIT: 1\n  WITH_PUSH: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release') || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) }}\n\nconcurrency:\n  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\n  cancel-in-progress: true\n\njobs:\n  build-docker:\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    environment: ${{ (github.event_name == 'push' && (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release') || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) && 'docker-build') || '' }}\n    runs-on: linux.9xlarge.ephemeral\n    strategy:\n      matrix:\n        tag: [\"cuda12.6\", \"cuda12.8\", \"cuda12.9\", \"cuda13.0\", \"rocm7.0\", \"rocm7.1\", \"cpu\"]\n    steps:\n      - name: Build docker image\n        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/binary-docker-build@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image-name: almalinux-builder\n          custom-tag-prefix: ${{matrix.tag}}\n          docker-build-dir: almalinux\n          DOCKER_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_TOKEN }}\n          DOCKER_ID: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_ID }}\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/.github/workflows/build-almalinux-images.yml",
    "numLines": 49,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 49
  },
  "type": "text"
}
47. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/build-manywheel-images.yml"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "name: Build manywheel docker images\n\non:\n  workflow_dispatch:\n  push:\n    branches:\n      - main\n      - release/*\n    tags:\n      # NOTE: Binary build pipelines should only get triggered on release candidate or nightly builds\n      # Release candidate tags look like: v1.11.0-rc1\n      - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+-rc[0-9]+\n    paths:\n      - .ci/docker/**\n      - .github/workflows/build-manywheel-images.yml\n      - .github/actions/binary-docker-build/**\n  pull_request:\n    paths:\n      - .ci/docker/**\n      - .github/workflows/build-manywheel-images.yml\n      - .github/actions/binary-docker-build/**\n\nenv:\n  DOCKER_REGISTRY: \"docker.io\"\n  DOCKER_BUILDKIT: 1\n  WITH_PUSH: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release') || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) }}\nconcurrency:\n  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\n  cancel-in-progress: true\n\njobs:\n  get-label-type:\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    name: get-label-type\n    uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n    with:\n      triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\n      issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\n      curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\n      curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\n\n  build:\n    environment: ${{ (github.event_name == 'push' && (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release') || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) && 'docker-build') || '' }}\n    needs: get-label-type\n    strategy:\n      fail-fast: false\n      matrix:\n        include: [\n          { name: \"manylinux2_28-builder\",          tag: \"cuda13.0\",          runner: \"linux.9xlarge.ephemeral\" },\n          { name: \"manylinux2_28-builder\",          tag: \"cuda12.8\",          runner: \"linux.9xlarge.ephemeral\" },\n          { name: \"manylinux2_28-builder\",          tag: \"cuda12.9\",          runner: \"linux.9xlarge.ephemeral\" },\n          { name: \"manylinux2_28-builder\",          tag: \"cuda12.6\",          runner: \"linux.9xlarge.ephemeral\" },\n          { name: \"manylinuxaarch64-builder\",       tag: \"cuda13.0\",          runner: \"linux.arm64.2xlarge.ephemeral\" },\n          { name: \"manylinuxaarch64-builder\",       tag: \"cuda12.9\",          runner: \"linux.arm64.2xlarge.ephemeral\" },\n          { name: \"manylinuxaarch64-builder\",       tag: \"cuda12.8\",          runner: \"linux.arm64.2xlarge.ephemeral\" },\n          { name: \"manylinuxaarch64-builder\",       tag: \"cuda12.6\",          runner: \"linux.arm64.2xlarge.ephemeral\" },\n          { name: \"manylinux2_28-builder\",          tag: \"rocm7.0\",           runner: \"linux.9xlarge.ephemeral\" },\n          { name: \"manylinux2_28-builder\",          tag: \"rocm7.1\",           runner: \"linux.9xlarge.ephemeral\" },\n          { name: \"manylinux2_28-builder\",          tag: \"cpu\",               runner: \"linux.9xlarge.ephemeral\" },\n          { name: \"manylinux2_28_aarch64-builder\",  tag: \"cpu-aarch64\",       runner: \"linux.arm64.2xlarge.ephemeral\" },\n          { name: \"manylinux2_28-builder\",          tag: \"xpu\",               runner: \"linux.9xlarge.ephemeral\" },\n        ]\n    runs-on: ${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}${{ matrix.runner }}\n    name: ${{ matrix.name }}:${{ matrix.tag }}\n    steps:\n      - name: Build docker image\n        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/binary-docker-build@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image-name: ${{ matrix.name }}\n          custom-tag-prefix: ${{ matrix.tag }}\n          docker-build-dir: manywheel\n          DOCKER_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_TOKEN }}\n          DOCKER_ID: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_ID }}\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/.github/workflows/build-manywheel-images.yml",
    "numLines": 74,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 74
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "description": "Find NCCL references in binary build matrix script"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "64:        \"nvidia-nccl-cu12==2.27.5; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n82:        \"nvidia-nccl-cu12==2.27.5; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n100:        \"nvidia-nccl-cu12==2.27.5; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n118:        \"nvidia-nccl-cu13==2.28.9; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n191:def get_nccl_wheel_version(arch_version: str) -> str:\n195:    return next(x for x in requirements if x.startswith(\"nvidia-nccl\")).split(\"==\")[1]\n198:def read_nccl_pin(arch_version: str) -> str:\n199:    nccl_pin_path = (\n204:        / f\"nccl-cu{arch_version[:2]}.txt\"\n206:    return nccl_pin_path.read_text().strip()\n209:def validate_nccl_dep_consistency(arch_version: str) -> None:\n210:    nccl_release_tag = read_nccl_pin(arch_version)\n211:    wheel_ver = get_nccl_wheel_version(arch_version)\n212:    if not nccl_release_tag.startswith(f\"v{wheel_ver}\"):\n214:            f\"{arch_version} NCCL release tag version {nccl_release_tag} \"\n464:    validate_nccl_dep_consistency(arch_version)"
}

93. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/docker-cache-rocm.yml"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "name: docker-cache-rocm\n\non:\n  workflow_run:\n    workflows: [docker-builds]\n    branches: [main, release]\n    types:\n      - completed\n  workflow_dispatch:\n    inputs:\n      branch:\n        type: string\n        description: Branch corresponding to the docker images being cached\n        required: true\n      run_id:\n        type: string\n        description: Workflow run id to pull artifacts from\n        required: true\n\nconcurrency:\n  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.workflow_run.head_branch || github.event.inputs.branch }}\n  cancel-in-progress: true\n\npermissions:\n  id-token: write\n  contents: read\n  actions: read\n\njobs:\n  download-docker-builds-artifacts:\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    name: download-docker-builds-artifacts\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    outputs:\n      pytorch-linux-jammy-rocm-n-py3: ${{ steps.process-artifacts.outputs.pytorch-linux-jammy-rocm-n-py3 }}\n      pytorch-linux-noble-rocm-n-py3: ${{ steps.process-artifacts.outputs.pytorch-linux-noble-rocm-n-py3 }}\n      pytorch-linux-jammy-rocm-n-py3-benchmarks: ${{ steps.process-artifacts.outputs.pytorch-linux-jammy-rocm-n-py3-benchmarks }}\n    steps:\n      - name: Download artifacts\n        uses: actions/download-artifact@65a9edc5881444af0b9093a5e628f2fe47ea3b2e #4.1.7\n        with:\n          run-id: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.id || github.event.inputs.run_id }}\n          path: ./docker-builds-artifacts\n          merge-multiple: true\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Process artifacts\n        id: process-artifacts\n        run: |\n          ls -R ./docker-builds-artifacts\n          cat ./docker-builds-artifacts/*txt >> \"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\"\n          cat \"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\"\n\n  docker-cache:\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    needs: download-docker-builds-artifacts\n    strategy:\n      fail-fast: false\n      matrix:\n        runner: [linux.rocm.gfx942.docker-cache, linux.rocm.mi250.docker-cache]\n        docker-image: [\n          \"${{ needs.download-docker-builds-artifacts.outputs.pytorch-linux-jammy-rocm-n-py3 }}\",\n          \"${{ needs.download-docker-builds-artifacts.outputs.pytorch-linux-noble-rocm-n-py3 }}\"\n          #\"${{ needs.download-docker-builds-artifacts.outputs.pytorch-linux-jammy-rocm-n-py3-benchmarks }}\"\n        ]\n    runs-on: \"${{ matrix.runner }}\"\n    steps:\n      - name: debug\n        run: |\n          JSON_STRINGIFIED=\"${{ toJSON(needs.download-docker-builds-artifacts.outputs) }}\"\n          echo \"Outputs of download-docker-builds-artifacts job: ${JSON_STRINGIFIED}\"\n\n      - name: configure aws credentials\n        id: aws_creds\n        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\n        with:\n          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\n          aws-region: us-east-1\n          role-duration-seconds: 18000\n\n      - name: Login to Amazon ECR\n        id: login-ecr\n        continue-on-error: false\n        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@062b18b96a7aff071d4dc91bc00c4c1a7945b076 # v2.0.1\n\n      - name: Generate ghrc.io tag\n        id: ghcr-io-tag\n        run: |\n            ecr_image=\"${{ matrix.docker-image }}\"\n            ghcr_image=\"ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${ecr_image##*:}\"\n            echo \"ghcr_image=${ghcr_image}\" >> \"$GITHUB_OUTPUT\"\n\n      - name: Pull docker image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image: ${{ steps.ghcr-io-tag.outputs.ghcr_image }}\n\n      - name: Save as tarball\n        run: |\n          docker_image_tag=${{ matrix.docker-image }}\n          docker_image_tag=\"${docker_image_tag#*:}\" # Remove everything before and including first \":\"\n          docker_image_tag=\"${docker_image_tag%-*}\" # Remove everything after and including last \"-\"\n          ref_name=${{ github.event.workflow_run.head_branch || github.event.inputs.branch }}\n          if [[ $ref_name =~ \"release/\" ]]; then\n            ref_suffix=\"release\"\n          elif [[ $ref_name == \"main\" ]]; then\n            ref_suffix=\"main\"\n          else\n            echo \"Unexpected branch in ref_name: ${ref_name}\" && exit 1\n          fi\n          docker tag ${{ steps.ghcr-io-tag.outputs.ghcr_image }} ${{ matrix.docker-image }}\n          # mv is atomic operation, so we use intermediate tar.tmp file to prevent read-write contention\n          docker save -o ~/pytorch-data/docker/${docker_image_tag}.tar.tmp ${{ matrix.docker-image }}\n          mv ~/pytorch-data/docker/${docker_image_tag}.tar.tmp ~/pytorch-data/docker/${docker_image_tag}_${ref_suffix}.tar\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/.github/workflows/docker-cache-rocm.yml",
    "numLines": 115,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 115
  },
  "type": "text"
}

TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\n      #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\n      DESIRED_CUDA: cu129\n      GPU_ARCH_VERSION: \"12.9\"\n      GPU_ARCH_TYPE: cuda\n      DOCKER_IMAGE: manylinux2_28-builder\n      DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX: cuda12.9\n      DESIRED_PYTHON: \"3.10\"\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build_name: manywheel-py3_10-cuda12_9\n      build_environment: linux-binary-manywheel\n      PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS: cuda-bindings==12.9.4; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-cuda-nvrtc-cu12==12.9.86; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-cuda-runtime-cu12==12.9.79; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-cuda-cupti-cu12==12.9.79; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-cudnn-cu12==9.10.2.21; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-cublas-cu12==12.9.1.4; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-cufft-cu12==11.4.1.4; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-curand-cu12==10.3.10.19; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-cusolver-cu12==11.7.5.82; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-cusparse-cu12==12.5.10.65; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-cusparselt-cu12==0.7.1; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-nccl-cu12==2.27.5; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-nvshmem-cu12==3.4.5; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-nvtx-cu12==12.9.79; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-nvjitlink-cu12==12.9.86; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-cufile-cu12==1.14.1.1; platform_system == 'Linux'\n    secrets:\n      github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n  manywheel-py3_10-cuda12_9-test:  # 12.8+ builds need sm_70+ runner\n    secrets:\n      github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n  manywheel-py3_10-cuda12_9-upload:  # Uploading\n    if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n    needs: manywheel-py3_10-cuda12_9-test\n    with:\n      PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch\n      PACKAGE_TYPE: manywheel\n      #     "content": "  libtorch-cuda12_9-shared-with-deps-release-build:\n    if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_binary-build-linux.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch\n      PACKAGE_TYPE: libtorch\n      # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\n      #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\n      DESIRED_CUDA: cu129\n      GPU_ARCH_VERSION: \"12.9\"\n      GPU_ARCH_TYPE: cuda\n      DOCKER_IMAGE: libtorch-cxx11-builder\n      DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX: cuda12.9\n      LIBTORCH_CONFIG: release\n      LIBTORCH_VARIANT: shared-with-deps\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build_name: libtorch-cuda12_9-shared-with-deps-release\n      build_environment: linux-binary-libtorch\n    secrets:\n      github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n  libtorch-cuda12_9-shared-with-deps-release-test:  # Testing\n    if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    needs:\n      - libtorch-cuda12_9-shared-with-deps-release-build\n      - get-label-type\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_binary-test-linux.yml\n    with:\n      PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch\n      PACKAGE_TYPE: libtorch\n      # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\n      #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\n      DESIRED_CUDA: cu129\n      GPU_ARCH_VERSION: \"12.9\"\n      GPU_ARCH_TYPE: cuda\n      DOCKER_IMAGE: libtorch-cxx11-builder\n      DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX: cuda12.9\n      LIBTORCH_CONFIG: release\n      LIBTORCH_VARIANT: shared-with-deps\n      build_name: libtorch-cuda12_9-shared-with-deps-release\n      build_environment: linux-binary-libtorch\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      runs_on: linux.g4dn.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu # 12.8+ builds need sm_70+ runner\n    secrets:\n      github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n  libtorch-cuda12_9-shared-with-deps-release-upload:  # Uploading\n    if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n    needs: libtorch-cuda12_9-shared-with-deps-release-test\n    with:\n      PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch\n      PACKAGE_TYPE: libtorch\n      # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\n      #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\n      DESIRED_CUDA: cu129\n      GPU_ARCH_VERSION: \"12.9\"\n      GPU_ARCH_TYPE: cuda\n      DOCKER_IMAGE: libtorch-cxx11-builder\n      DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX: cuda12.9\n      LIBTORCH_CONFIG: release\n      LIBTORCH_VARIANT: shared-with-deps\n      build_name: libtorch-cuda12_9-shared-with-deps-release\n    secrets:\n      github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_binary-upload.yml\n\n  libtorch-cuda13_0-shared-with-deps-release-build:\n    if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_binary-build-linux.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch\n      PACKAGE_TYPE: libtorch\n      # }
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,

111. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/scripts/generate_binary_build_matrix.py",
  "limit": 150,
  "offset": 270
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "        \"rocm\": f\"rocm{gpu_arch_version}\",\n        \"xpu\": \"xpu\",\n    }.get(gpu_arch_type, gpu_arch_version)\n\n\ndef list_without(in_list: list[str], without: list[str]) -> list[str]:\n    return [item for item in in_list if item not in without]\n\n\ndef generate_libtorch_matrix(\n    os: str,\n    release_type: str,\n    arches: Optional[list[str]] = None,\n    libtorch_variants: Optional[list[str]] = None,\n) -> list[dict[str, str]]:\n    if arches is None:\n        arches = [\"cpu\"]\n        if os == \"linux\":\n            arches += CUDA_ARCHES\n            arches += ROCM_ARCHES\n        elif os == \"windows\":\n            # TODO (huydhn): Only build CUDA 12.9 for Linux. This logic is to be cleaned up\n            # in 2.10\n            windows_cuda_arches = CUDA_ARCHES.copy()\n            windows_cuda_arches.remove(\"12.9\")\n            arches += windows_cuda_arches\n    if libtorch_variants is None:\n        libtorch_variants = [\n            \"shared-with-deps\",\n            \"shared-without-deps\",\n            \"static-with-deps\",\n            \"static-without-deps\",\n        ]\n\n    ret: list[dict[str, str]] = []\n    for arch_version in arches:\n        for libtorch_variant in libtorch_variants:\n            gpu_arch_type = arch_type(arch_version)\n            gpu_arch_version = \"\" if arch_version == \"cpu\" else arch_version\n            # ROCm builds without-deps failed even in ROCm runners; skip for now\n            if gpu_arch_type == \"rocm\" and (\"without-deps\" in libtorch_variant):\n                continue\n            ret.append(\n                {\n                    \"gpu_arch_type\": gpu_arch_type,\n                    \"gpu_arch_version\": gpu_arch_version,\n                    \"desired_cuda\": translate_desired_cuda(\n                        gpu_arch_type, gpu_arch_version\n                    ),\n                    \"libtorch_config\": release_type,\n                    \"libtorch_variant\": libtorch_variant,\n                    \"container_image\": (\n                        LIBTORCH_CONTAINER_IMAGES[arch_version].split(\":\")[0]\n                        if os not in (\"windows\", \"windows-arm64\")\n                        else \"\"\n                    ),\n                    \"container_image_tag_prefix\": (\n                        LIBTORCH_CONTAINER_IMAGES[arch_version].split(\":\")[1]\n                        if os not in (\"windows\", \"windows-arm64\")\n                        else \"\"\n                    ),\n                    \"package_type\": \"libtorch\",\n                    \"build_name\": f\"libtorch-{gpu_arch_type}{gpu_arch_version}-{libtorch_variant}-{release_type}\".replace(\n                        \".\", \"_\"\n                    ),\n                }\n            )\n    return ret\n\n\ndef generate_wheels_matrix(\n    os: str,\n    arches: Optional[list[str]] = None,\n    python_versions: Optional[list[str]] = None,\n) -> list[dict[str, str]]:\n    package_type = \"wheel\"\n    if os == \"linux\" or os == \"linux-aarch64\" or os == \"linux-s390x\":\n        # NOTE: We only build manywheel packages for x86_64 and aarch64 and s390x linux\n        package_type = \"manywheel\"\n\n    if python_versions is None:\n        python_versions = FULL_PYTHON_VERSIONS\n\n    if arches is None:\n        # Define default compute archivectures\n        arches = [\"cpu\"]\n        if os == \"linux\":\n            arches += CUDA_ARCHES + ROCM_ARCHES + XPU_ARCHES\n        elif os == \"windows\":\n            # TODO (huydhn): Only build CUDA 12.9 for Linux. This logic is to be cleaned up\n            # in 2.10\n            windows_cuda_arches = CUDA_ARCHES.copy()\n            windows_cuda_arches.remove(\"12.9\")\n            arches += windows_cuda_arches + XPU_ARCHES\n        elif os == \"linux-aarch64\":\n            # Separate new if as the CPU type is different and\n            # uses different build/test scripts\n            arches = CPU_AARCH64_ARCH + CUDA_AARCH64_ARCHES\n        elif os == \"linux-s390x\":\n            # Only want the one arch as the CPU type is different and\n            # uses different build/test scripts\n            arches = [\"cpu-s390x\"]\n\n    ret: list[dict[str, str]] = []\n    for python_version in python_versions:\n        for arch_version in arches:\n            gpu_arch_type = arch_type(arch_version)\n            gpu_arch_version = (\n                \"\"\n                if arch_version == \"cpu\"\n                or arch_version == \"cpu-aarch64\"\n                or arch_version == \"cpu-s390x\"\n                or arch_version == \"xpu\"\n                else arch_version\n            )\n\n            # TODO: Enable python 3.14 for rest\n            if os not in [\n                \"linux\",\n                \"linux-aarch64\",\n                \"linux-s390x\",\n                \"macos-arm64\",\n                \"windows\",\n            ] and (python_version == \"3.14\" or python_version == \"3.14t\"):\n                continue\n\n            # cuda linux wheels require PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS to install\n\n            if (\n                arch_version in [\"13.0\", \"12.9\", \"12.8\", \"12.6\"]\n                and os == \"linux\"\n                or arch_version in CUDA_AARCH64_ARCHES\n            ):\n                desired_cuda = translate_desired_cuda(gpu_arch_type, gpu_arch_version)\n                ret.append(\n                    {\n                        \"python_version\": python_version,\n                        \"gpu_arch_type\": gpu_arch_type,\n                        \"gpu_arch_version\": gpu_arch_version,\n                        \"desired_cuda\": desired_cuda,\n                        \"container_image\": WHEEL_CONTAINER_IMAGES[arch_version].split(\n                            \":\"\n                        )[0],\n                        \"container_image_tag_prefix\": WHEEL_CONTAINER_IMAGES[\n                            arch_version\n                        ].split(\":\")[1],\n                        \"package_type\": package_type,\n                        \"pytorch_extra_install_requirements\": (\n                            PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS[\n                                f\"{desired_cuda[2:4]}.{desired_cuda[4:]}\"  # for cuda-aarch64: cu126 -> 12.6",
    "filePath": "/workspace/.github/scripts/generate_binary_build_matrix.py",
    "numLines": 150,
    "startLine": 270,
    "totalLines": 473
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/generated-linux-binary-manywheel-nightly.yml",
  "limit": 120,
  "offset": 381
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "  manywheel-py3_10-rocm7_0-build:\n    if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_binary-build-linux.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch\n      PACKAGE_TYPE: manywheel\n      # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\n      #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\n      DESIRED_CUDA: rocm7.0\n      GPU_ARCH_VERSION: \"7.0\"\n      GPU_ARCH_TYPE: rocm\n      DOCKER_IMAGE: manylinux2_28-builder\n      DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX: rocm7.0\n      DESIRED_PYTHON: \"3.10\"\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      timeout-minutes: 300\n      build_name: manywheel-py3_10-rocm7_0\n      build_environment: linux-binary-manywheel\n    secrets:\n      github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n  manywheel-py3_10-rocm7_0-test:  # Testing\n    if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    needs:\n      - manywheel-py3_10-rocm7_0-build\n      - get-label-type\n    runs-on: linux.rocm.gpu.mi250.1\n    timeout-minutes: 240\n    env:\n      PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch\n      PACKAGE_TYPE: manywheel\n      # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\n      #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\n      DESIRED_CUDA: rocm7.0\n      GPU_ARCH_VERSION: \"7.0\"\n      GPU_ARCH_TYPE: rocm\n      SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1\n      DOCKER_IMAGE: manylinux2_28-builder\n      DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX: rocm7.0\n      DESIRED_PYTHON: \"3.10\"\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n    steps:\n      - name: Setup ROCm\n        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-rocm\n      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v4.1.7\n        name: Download Build Artifacts\n        with:\n          name: manywheel-py3_10-rocm7_0\n          path: \"${{ runner.temp }}/artifacts/\"\n      - name: Checkout PyTorch\n        uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          submodules: recursive\n          path: pytorch\n          show-progress: false\n      - name: Clean PyTorch checkout\n        run: |\n          # Remove any artifacts from the previous checkouts\n          git clean -fxd\n        working-directory: pytorch\n      - name: ROCm set GPU_FLAG\n        run: |\n          echo \"GPU_FLAG=--device=/dev/mem --device=/dev/kfd --device=/dev/dri --group-add video --group-add daemon\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n      - name: configure aws credentials\n        id: aws_creds\n        if: ${{ startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') }}\n        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4\n        with:\n          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\n          aws-region: us-east-1\n          role-duration-seconds: 18000\n      - name: Calculate docker image\n        id: calculate-docker-image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-registry: ${{ startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') && '308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com' || 'docker.io' }}\n          docker-image-name: manylinux2_28-builder\n          custom-tag-prefix: rocm7.0\n          docker-build-dir: .ci/docker\n          working-directory: pytorch\n      - name: Pull Docker image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n      - name: Test Pytorch binary\n        uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/test-pytorch-binary\n        env:\n          DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n      - name: Teardown ROCm\n        uses: ./.github/actions/teardown-rocm\n  manywheel-py3_10-rocm7_0-upload:  # Uploading\n    if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n    needs: manywheel-py3_10-rocm7_0-test\n    with:\n      PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch\n      PACKAGE_TYPE: manywheel\n      # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\n      #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\n      DESIRED_CUDA: rocm7.0\n      GPU_ARCH_VERSION: \"7.0\"\n      GPU_ARCH_TYPE: rocm\n      DOCKER_IMAGE: manylinux2_28-builder\n      DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX: rocm7.0\n      DESIRED_PYTHON: \"3.10\"\n      build_name: manywheel-py3_10-rocm7_0\n    secrets:\n      github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_binary-upload.yml\n\n  manywheel-py3_10-rocm7_1-build:\n    if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_binary-build-linux.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:",

    "numLines": 120,

114. Bash @ -

{
  "command": "grep -rn \"nightly\" /workspace/.github/workflows/ --include=\"*.yml\" | grep -v \"generated-\" | grep -v \"ciflow/nightly\\|nightly-s3\\|inductor-nightly\\|docker-release\\|build-triton-wheel\\|build-vllm\\|binary.*nightly\\|nightly.yml\\|#.*nightly\\|scheduler.*nightly\\|tag.*nightly\" | head -30",

}

Input
{
  "command": "grep -n \"ROCM_ARCHES\\|rocm\" /workspace/.github/scripts/generate_binary_build_matrix.py | head -30",
  "description": "Find ROCm config in binary build matrix script"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "40:ROCM_ARCHES = [\"7.0\", \"7.1\"]\n170:    f\"rocm-{major}.{minor}\": dict(\n171:        name=f\"rocm-{major}.{minor}\",\n172:        index_url=f\"{PYTORCH_NIGHTLY_PIP_INDEX_URL}/rocm{major}.{minor}\",\n174:        accelerator=\"rocm\",\n176:    for major, minor in (map(int, version.split(\".\")) for version in ROCM_ARCHES)\n222:    elif arch_version in ROCM_ARCHES:\n223:        return \"rocm\"\n244:    **{gpu_arch: f\"manylinux2_28-builder:rocm{gpu_arch}\" for gpu_arch in ROCM_ARCHES},\n256:    **{gpu_arch: f\"libtorch-cxx11-builder:rocm{gpu_arch}\" for gpu_arch in ROCM_ARCHES},\n270:        \"rocm\": f\"rocm{gpu_arch_version}\",\n289:            arches += ROCM_ARCHES\n310:            if gpu_arch_type == \"rocm\" and (\"without-deps\" in libtorch_variant):\n357:            arches += CUDA_ARCHES + ROCM_ARCHES + XPU_ARCHES"
}
119. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/scripts/generate_binary_build_matrix.py",
  "limit": 50,
  "offset": 1
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\n\"\"\"Generates a matrix to be utilized through github actions\n\nWill output a condensed version of the matrix if on a pull request that only\nincludes the latest version of python we support built on three different\narchitectures:\n    * CPU\n    * Latest CUDA\n    * Latest ROCM\n    * Latest XPU\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nimport os\nimport re\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional\n\n\nSCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).absolute().parent\nREPO_ROOT = SCRIPT_DIR.parent.parent\n\n\nCUDA_ARCHES = [\"12.6\", \"12.8\", \"12.9\", \"13.0\"]\nCUDA_STABLE = \"12.8\"\nCUDA_ARCHES_FULL_VERSION = {\n    \"12.6\": \"12.6.3\",\n    \"12.8\": \"12.8.1\",\n    \"12.9\": \"12.9.1\",\n    \"13.0\": \"13.0.0\",\n}\nCUDA_ARCHES_CUDNN_VERSION = {\n    \"12.6\": \"9\",\n    \"12.8\": \"9\",\n    \"12.9\": \"9\",\n    \"13.0\": \"9\",\n}\n\nROCM_ARCHES = [\"7.0\", \"7.1\"]\n\nXPU_ARCHES = [\"xpu\"]\n\nCPU_AARCH64_ARCH = [\"cpu-aarch64\"]\n\nCPU_S390X_ARCH = [\"cpu-s390x\"]\n\nCUDA_AARCH64_ARCHES = [\"12.6-aarch64\", \"12.8-aarch64\", \"12.9-aarch64\", \"13.0-aarch64\"]\n\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/.github/scripts/generate_binary_build_matrix.py",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 473
  },
  "type": "text"
}
120. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "grep -B2 -A20 \"_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\" /workspace/.github/workflows/trunk.yml | head -30",
  "description": "View runner determinator call in trunk.yml"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "  get-label-type:\n    name: get-label-type\n    uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n    if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    with:\n      triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\n      issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\n      curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\n      curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\n\n  libtorch-linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-debug-build:\n    name: libtorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-debug\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      build-environment: libtorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11\n      build-generates-artifacts: false\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      runner: \"linux.c7i.4xlarge\"\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1 },"
}

Input
{
  "command": "grep -B2 -A20 \"_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\" /workspace/.github/workflows/generated-linux-binary-manywheel-nightly.yml | head -30",
  "description": "View runner determinator call in generated workflow"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    name: get-label-type\n    uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n    with:\n      triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\n      issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\n      curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\n      curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\n  manywheel-py3_10-cpu-build:\n    if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_binary-build-linux.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch\n      PACKAGE_TYPE: manywheel\n      # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\n      #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\n      DESIRED_CUDA: cpu\n      GPU_ARCH_TYPE: cpu\n      DOCKER_IMAGE: manylinux2_28-builder\n      DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX: cpu\n      DESIRED_PYTHON: \"3.10\"\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\""
}

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/scripts/generate_binary_build_matrix.py",
  "limit": 100,
  "offset": 150
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "# Used by tools/nightly.py\nPYTORCH_NIGHTLY_PIP_INDEX_URL = \"https://download.pytorch.org/whl/nightly\"\nNIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX = {\n    \"cpu\": dict(\n        name=\"cpu\",\n        index_url=f\"{PYTORCH_NIGHTLY_PIP_INDEX_URL}/cpu\",\n        supported_platforms=[\"Linux\", \"macOS\", \"Windows\"],\n        accelerator=\"cpu\",\n    )\n}\nCUDA_NIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX = {\n    f\"cuda-{major}.{minor}\": dict(\n        name=f\"cuda-{major}.{minor}\",\n        index_url=f\"{PYTORCH_NIGHTLY_PIP_INDEX_URL}/cu{major}{minor}\",\n        supported_platforms=[\"Linux\", \"Windows\"],\n        accelerator=\"cuda\",\n    )\n    for major, minor in (map(int, version.split(\".\")) for version in CUDA_ARCHES)\n}\nROCM_NIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX = {\n    f\"rocm-{major}.{minor}\": dict(\n        name=f\"rocm-{major}.{minor}\",\n        index_url=f\"{PYTORCH_NIGHTLY_PIP_INDEX_URL}/rocm{major}.{minor}\",\n        supported_platforms=[\"Linux\"],\n        accelerator=\"rocm\",\n    )\n    for major, minor in (map(int, version.split(\".\")) for version in ROCM_ARCHES)\n}\nXPU_NIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX = {\n    \"xpu\": dict(\n        name=\"xpu\",\n        index_url=f\"{PYTORCH_NIGHTLY_PIP_INDEX_URL}/xpu\",\n        supported_platforms=[\"Linux\"],\n        accelerator=\"xpu\",\n    )\n}\nNIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX.update(CUDA_NIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX)\nNIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX.update(ROCM_NIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX)\nNIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX.update(XPU_NIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX)\n\n\ndef get_nccl_wheel_version(arch_version: str) -> str:\n    requirements = map(\n        str.strip, re.split(\"[;|]\", PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS[arch_version])\n    )\n    return next(x for x in requirements if x.startswith(\"nvidia-nccl\")).split(\"==\")[1]\n\n\ndef read_nccl_pin(arch_version: str) -> str:\n    nccl_pin_path = (\n        REPO_ROOT\n        / \".ci\"\n        / \"docker\"\n        / \"ci_commit_pins\"\n        / f\"nccl-cu{arch_version[:2]}.txt\"\n    )\n    return nccl_pin_path.read_text().strip()\n\n\ndef validate_nccl_dep_consistency(arch_version: str) -> None:\n    nccl_release_tag = read_nccl_pin(arch_version)\n    wheel_ver = get_nccl_wheel_version(arch_version)\n    if not nccl_release_tag.startswith(f\"v{wheel_ver}\"):\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            f\"{arch_version} NCCL release tag version {nccl_release_tag} \"\n            f\"does not correspond to wheel version {wheel_ver}\"\n        )\n\n\ndef arch_type(arch_version: str) -> str:\n    if arch_version in CUDA_ARCHES:\n        return \"cuda\"\n    elif arch_version in ROCM_ARCHES:\n        return \"rocm\"\n    elif arch_version in XPU_ARCHES:\n        return \"xpu\"\n    elif arch_version in CPU_AARCH64_ARCH:\n        return \"cpu-aarch64\"\n    elif arch_version in CPU_S390X_ARCH:\n        return \"cpu-s390x\"\n    elif arch_version in CUDA_AARCH64_ARCHES:\n        return \"cuda-aarch64\"\n    else:  # arch_version should always be \"cpu\" in this case\n        return \"cpu\"\n\n\nDEFAULT_TAG = os.getenv(\"RELEASE_VERSION_TAG\", \"main\")\n\nWHEEL_CONTAINER_IMAGES = {\n    **{gpu_arch: f\"manylinux2_28-builder:cuda{gpu_arch}\" for gpu_arch in CUDA_ARCHES},\n    **{\n        gpu_arch: f\"manylinuxaarch64-builder:cuda{gpu_arch.replace('-aarch64', '')}\"\n        for gpu_arch in CUDA_AARCH64_ARCHES\n    },\n    **{gpu_arch: f\"manylinux2_28-builder:rocm{gpu_arch}\" for gpu_arch in ROCM_ARCHES},\n    \"xpu\": \"manylinux2_28-builder:xpu\",\n    \"cpu\": \"manylinux2_28-builder:cpu\",\n    \"cpu-aarch64\": \"manylinux2_28_aarch64-builder:cpu-aarch64\",\n    \"cpu-s390x\": \"pytorch/manylinuxs390x-builder:cpu-s390x\",\n}",
    "filePath": "/workspace/.github/scripts/generate_binary_build_matrix.py",
    "numLines": 100,
    "startLine": 150,
    "totalLines": 473
  },
  "type": "text"
}

134. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat /workspace/.github/templates/linux_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2 | head -60",
  "description": "View Linux binary build workflow template"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "{% import 'common.yml.j2' as common %}\n{% import 'upload.yml.j2' as upload %}\n\n{%- block name -%}\n# Template is at:    .github/templates/linux_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2\n# Generation script: .github/scripts/generate_ci_workflows.py\nname: !{{ build_environment }}\n{%- endblock %}\n\n\non:\n  push:\n    {%- if branches == \"nightly\" %}\n    # NOTE: Meta Employees can trigger new nightlies using: https://fburl.com/trigger_pytorch_nightly_build\n    {%- endif %}\n    branches:\n      - !{{ branches }}\n    {%- if branches == \"nightly\" %}\n    tags:\n      # NOTE: Binary build pipelines should only get triggered on release candidate builds\n      # Release candidate tags look like: v1.11.0-rc1\n      - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+-rc[0-9]+\n    {%- endif %}\n{%- for label in ciflow_config.labels | sort %}\n    {%- if loop.first and branches != \"nightly\" %}\n    tags:\n    {%- endif %}\n      - '!{{ label }}/*'\n{%- endfor %}\n  workflow_dispatch:\n\npermissions:\n  id-token: write\n\nenv:\n  # Needed for conda builds\n  {%- if \"aarch64\" in build_environment %}\n  ALPINE_IMAGE: \"arm64v8/alpine\"\n  {%- elif \"s390x\" in build_environment %}\n  ALPINE_IMAGE: \"docker.io/s390x/alpine\"\n  {%- else %}\n  ALPINE_IMAGE: \"308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine\"\n  {%- endif %}\n  AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1\n  BINARY_ENV_FILE: /tmp/env\n  BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: !{{ build_environment }}\n  GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n  PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n  PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR: /artifacts\n  PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch\n  SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n  SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 0\n!{{ common.concurrency(build_environment) }}\n\njobs:\n  get-label-type:\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    name: get-label-type\n    uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n    with:"
}
135. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat /workspace/.github/templates/common.yml.j2",
  "description": "View common Jinja2 template"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "{%- set upload_artifact_s3_action = \"seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@v5\" -%}\n{%- set download_artifact_s3_action = \"seemethere/download-artifact-s3@v4\" -%}\n{%- set upload_artifact_action = \"actions/upload-artifact@v4.4.0\" -%}\n{%- set download_artifact_action = \"actions/download-artifact@v4.1.7\" -%}\n\n{%- set timeout_minutes = 240 -%}\n{%- set timeout_minutes_windows_binary = 360 -%}\n\n{%- macro concurrency(build_environment) -%}\nconcurrency:\n  group: !{{ build_environment }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\n  cancel-in-progress: true\n{%- endmacro -%}\n\n{%- macro display_ec2_information() -%}\n      - name: Display EC2 information\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          set -euo pipefail\n          function get_ec2_metadata() {\n            # Pulled from instance metadata endpoint for EC2\n            # see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/instancedata-data-retrieval.html\n            category=$1\n            curl -H \"X-aws-ec2-metadata-token: $(curl -s -X PUT \"http://169.254.169.254/latest/api/token\" -H \"X-aws-ec2-metadata-token-ttl-seconds: 30\")\" -fsSL \"http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/${category}\"\n          }\n          echo \"ami-id: $(get_ec2_metadata ami-id)\"\n          echo \"instance-id: $(get_ec2_metadata instance-id)\"\n          echo \"instance-type: $(get_ec2_metadata instance-type)\"\n          echo \"system info $(uname -a)\"\n{%- endmacro -%}\n\n{%- macro setup_ec2_windows() -%}\n      !{{ display_ec2_information() }}\n      - name: \"[FB EMPLOYEES] Enable SSH (Click me for login details)\"\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\n        continue-on-error: true\n        with:\n          github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n      - name: Enable git long paths and symlinks on Windows and disable fsmonitor daemon\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          git config --global core.longpaths true\n          git config --global core.symlinks true\n\n          # https://git-scm.com/docs/git-fsmonitor--daemon.  The daemon could lock\n          # the directory on Windows and prevent GHA from checking out as reported\n          # in https://github.com/actions/checkout/issues/1018\n          git config --global core.fsmonitor false\n      # Needed for binary builds, see: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/73339#issuecomment-1058981560\n      - name: Enable long paths on Windows\n        shell: powershell\n        run: |\n          Set-ItemProperty -Path \"HKLM:\\\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\FileSystem\" -Name \"LongPathsEnabled\" -Value 1\n      # Since it's just a defensive command, the workflow should continue even the command fails. This step can be\n      # removed once Windows Defender is removed from the AMI\n      - name: Disables Windows Defender scheduled and real-time scanning for files in directories used by PyTorch\n        continue-on-error: true\n        shell: powershell\n        run: |\n          Add-MpPreference -ExclusionPath $(Get-Location).tostring(),$Env:TEMP -ErrorAction Ignore\n          # Let's both exclude the path and disable Windows Defender completely just to be sure\n          # that it doesn't interfere\n          Set-MpPreference -DisableRealtimeMonitoring $True -ErrorAction Ignore\n{%- endmacro -%}\n\n{%- macro apply_filter() -%}\n      - name: Check if the job is disabled\n        id: filter\n        # Binary workflows checkout to pytorch subdirectory instead\n        uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/filter-test-configs\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          # NB: Use a mock test matrix with a default value here. After filtering, if the\n          # returned matrix is empty, it means that the job is disabled\n          test-matrix: |\n            { include: [\n              { config: \"default\" },\n            ]}\n{%- endmacro -%}\n\n{%- macro checkout(submodules=\"recursive\", deep_clone=True, directory=\"\", repository=\"pytorch/pytorch\", branch=\"\", checkout_pr_head=True) -%}\n      - name: Checkout !{{ 'PyTorch' if repository == \"pytorch/pytorch\" else repository }}\n        uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n      {%- if branch %}\n          ref: !{{ branch }}\n      {%- elif checkout_pr_head %}\n          ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n      {%- endif %}\n      {%- if deep_clone %}\n          # deep clone, to allow use of git merge-base\n          fetch-depth: 0\n      {%- endif %}\n          submodules: !{{ submodules }}\n      {%- if repository != \"pytorch/pytorch\" %}\n          repository: !{{ repository }}\n      {%- endif %}\n      {%- if directory %}\n          path: !{{ directory }}\n      {%- endif %}\n          show-progress: false\n      - name: Clean !{{ 'PyTorch' if repository == \"pytorch/pytorch\" else repository }} checkout\n        run: |\n          # Remove any artifacts from the previous checkouts\n          git clean -fxd\n      {%- if directory%}\n        working-directory: !{{ directory }}\n      {%- endif %}\n{%- endmacro -%}\n\n{%- macro wait_and_kill_ssh_windows(pytorch_directory=\"\") -%}\n      - name: Wait until all sessions have drained\n        shell: powershell\n{%- if pytorch_directory %}\n        working-directory: !{{ pytorch_directory }}\n{%- endif %}\n        if: always()\n        timeout-minutes: 120\n        run: |\n          .github\\scripts\\wait_for_ssh_to_drain.ps1\n      - name: Kill active ssh sessions if still around (Useful if workflow was cancelled)\n        shell: powershell\n{%- if pytorch_directory %}\n        working-directory: !{{ pytorch_directory }}\n{%- endif %}\n        if: always()\n        run: |\n          .github\\scripts\\kill_active_ssh_sessions.ps1\n{%- endmacro -%}"
}
136. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "tail -100 /workspace/.github/scripts/generate_binary_build_matrix.py",
  "description": "View end of binary build matrix script"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "    ret: list[dict[str, str]] = []\n    for python_version in python_versions:\n        for arch_version in arches:\n            gpu_arch_type = arch_type(arch_version)\n            gpu_arch_version = (\n                \"\"\n                if arch_version == \"cpu\"\n                or arch_version == \"cpu-aarch64\"\n                or arch_version == \"cpu-s390x\"\n                or arch_version == \"xpu\"\n                else arch_version\n            )\n\n            # TODO: Enable python 3.14 for rest\n            if os not in [\n                \"linux\",\n                \"linux-aarch64\",\n                \"linux-s390x\",\n                \"macos-arm64\",\n                \"windows\",\n            ] and (python_version == \"3.14\" or python_version == \"3.14t\"):\n                continue\n\n            # cuda linux wheels require PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS to install\n\n            if (\n                arch_version in [\"13.0\", \"12.9\", \"12.8\", \"12.6\"]\n                and os == \"linux\"\n                or arch_version in CUDA_AARCH64_ARCHES\n            ):\n                desired_cuda = translate_desired_cuda(gpu_arch_type, gpu_arch_version)\n                ret.append(\n                    {\n                        \"python_version\": python_version,\n                        \"gpu_arch_type\": gpu_arch_type,\n                        \"gpu_arch_version\": gpu_arch_version,\n                        \"desired_cuda\": desired_cuda,\n                        \"container_image\": WHEEL_CONTAINER_IMAGES[arch_version].split(\n                            \":\"\n                        )[0],\n                        \"container_image_tag_prefix\": WHEEL_CONTAINER_IMAGES[\n                            arch_version\n                        ].split(\":\")[1],\n                        \"package_type\": package_type,\n                        \"pytorch_extra_install_requirements\": (\n                            PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS[\n                                f\"{desired_cuda[2:4]}.{desired_cuda[4:]}\"  # for cuda-aarch64: cu126 -> 12.6\n                            ]\n                            if os == \"linux-aarch64\"\n                            else PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS[arch_version]\n                        ),\n                        \"build_name\": (\n                            f\"{package_type}-py{python_version}-{gpu_arch_type}\"\n                            f\"{'-' if 'aarch64' in gpu_arch_type else ''}{gpu_arch_version.replace('-aarch64', '')}\".replace(\n                                \".\", \"_\"\n                            )\n                        ),  # include special case for aarch64 build, remove the -aarch64 postfix\n                    }\n                )\n            else:\n                ret.append(\n                    {\n                        \"python_version\": python_version,\n                        \"gpu_arch_type\": gpu_arch_type,\n                        \"gpu_arch_version\": gpu_arch_version,\n                        \"desired_cuda\": translate_desired_cuda(\n                            gpu_arch_type, gpu_arch_version\n                        ),\n                        \"container_image\": WHEEL_CONTAINER_IMAGES[arch_version].split(\n                            \":\"\n                        )[0],\n                        \"container_image_tag_prefix\": WHEEL_CONTAINER_IMAGES[\n                            arch_version\n                        ].split(\":\")[1],\n                        \"package_type\": package_type,\n                        \"build_name\": f\"{package_type}-py{python_version}-{gpu_arch_type}{gpu_arch_version}\".replace(\n                            \".\", \"_\"\n                        ),\n                        \"pytorch_extra_install_requirements\": (\n                            PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS[\"xpu\"]\n                            if gpu_arch_type == \"xpu\"\n                            else \"\"\n                        ),\n                    }\n                )\n\n    return ret\n\n\narch_version = \"\"\nfor arch_version in CUDA_ARCHES:\n    validate_nccl_dep_consistency(arch_version)\ndel arch_version\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    # Used by tools/nightly.py\n    (SCRIPT_DIR / \"nightly_source_matrix.json\").write_text(\n        json.dumps(NIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX, indent=4) + \"\\n\"\n    )"
}

145. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat /workspace/.github/actions/setup-rocm/action.yml",
  "description": "View setup-rocm action"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "name: Setup ROCm host\n\ndescription: Set up ROCm host for CI\n\nruns:\n  using: composite\n  steps:\n    - name: Runner ROCm version\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        dpkg -l | grep -E \"  rocm\"\n\n    - name: Stop all running docker containers\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        # ignore expansion of \"docker ps -q\" since it could be empty\n        # shellcheck disable=SC2046\n        docker stop $(docker ps -q) || true\n        # Prune all stopped containers.\n        docker container prune -f\n\n    - name: Runner health check system info\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        cat /etc/os-release || true\n        cat /etc/apt/sources.list.d/rocm.list || true\n        cat /opt/rocm/.info/version || true\n        whoami\n\n    - name: Runner health check amdgpu info\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        dpkg -l | grep -E \"  amdgpu\"\n\n    - name: Runner health check rocm-smi\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        rocm-smi\n\n    - name: Runner health check rocminfo\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        rocminfo\n\n    - name: Runner health check GPU count\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        ngpu=$(rocminfo | grep -c -E 'Name:.*\\sgfx')\n        msg=\"Please file an issue on pytorch/pytorch reporting the faulty runner. Include a link to the runner logs so the runner can be identified\"\n        if [[ $ngpu -eq 0 ]]; then\n            echo \"Error: Failed to detect any GPUs on the runner\"\n            echo \"$msg\"\n            exit 1\n        fi\n\n    - name: Runner diskspace health check\n      uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/diskspace-cleanup@main\n      if: always()\n\n    - name: Runner health check disconnect on failure\n      if: ${{ failure() }}\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        killall runsvc.sh\n\n    - name: Setup useful environment variables\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        RUNNER_ARTIFACT_DIR=\"${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts\"\n        rm -rf \"${RUNNER_ARTIFACT_DIR}\"\n        mkdir -p \"${RUNNER_ARTIFACT_DIR}\"\n        echo \"RUNNER_ARTIFACT_DIR=${RUNNER_ARTIFACT_DIR}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n\n        RUNNER_TEST_RESULTS_DIR=\"${RUNNER_TEMP}/test-results\"\n        rm -rf \"${RUNNER_TEST_RESULTS_DIR}\"\n        mkdir -p \"${RUNNER_TEST_RESULTS_DIR}\"\n        echo \"RUNNER_TEST_RESULTS_DIR=${RUNNER_TEST_RESULTS_DIR}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n\n        RUNNER_DOCS_DIR=\"${RUNNER_TEMP}/docs\"\n        rm -rf \"${RUNNER_DOCS_DIR}\"\n        mkdir -p \"${RUNNER_DOCS_DIR}\"\n        echo \"RUNNER_DOCS_DIR=${RUNNER_DOCS_DIR}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n\n    - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        env | grep '^GITHUB' >> \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n        env | grep '^CI' >> \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n\n    - name: ROCm set GPU_FLAG\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        # All GPUs are visible to the runner; visibility, if needed, will be set by run_test.py.\n        # Add render group for container creation.\n        render_gid=`cat /etc/group | grep render | cut -d: -f3`\n        # Ensure GPU isolation if pod is part of kubernetes setup with DEVICE_FLAG.\n        if [ -f \"/etc/podinfo/gha-render-devices\" ]; then\n          DEVICE_FLAG=$(cat /etc/podinfo/gha-render-devices)\n        else\n          DEVICE_FLAG=\"--device /dev/dri\"\n        fi\n        # The --group-add daemon and --group-add bin are needed in the Ubuntu 24.04 and Almalinux OSs respectively.\n        # This is due to the device files (/dev/kfd & /dev/dri) being owned by video group on bare metal.\n        # This video group ID maps to subgid 1 inside the docker image due to the /etc/subgid entries.\n        # The group name corresponding to group ID 1 can change depending on the OS, so both are necessary.\n        echo \"GPU_FLAG=--device=/dev/mem --device=/dev/kfd $DEVICE_FLAG --group-add video --group-add $render_gid --group-add daemon --group-add bin --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --network=host\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n\n    - name: configure aws credentials\n      id: aws_creds\n      uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\n      with:\n        role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\n        aws-region: us-east-1\n        role-duration-seconds: 18000\n\n    - name: Login to Amazon ECR\n      id: login-ecr\n      continue-on-error: true\n      uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@062b18b96a7aff071d4dc91bc00c4c1a7945b076 # v2.0.1\n\n    - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        env | grep '^GITHUB' >> \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n        env | grep '^CI' >> \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n        env | grep '^RUNNER' >> \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\""
}

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/docker-builds.yml",
  "limit": 50,
  "offset": 45
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "    needs: get-label-type\n    timeout-minutes: 240\n    strategy:\n      fail-fast: false\n      matrix:\n        runner: [linux.12xlarge]\n        docker-image-name: [\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda13.0-cudnn9-py3-gcc11,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3.12-gcc11-vllm,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11-inductor-benchmarks,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda13.0-cudnn9-py3-gcc11-inductor-benchmarks,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.4-cudnn9-py3-gcc11,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.10-clang12,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.11-clang12,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.12-clang12,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.13-clang12,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.14-clang12,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-rocm-n-py3,\n          pytorch-linux-noble-rocm-n-py3,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-rocm-n-py3-benchmarks,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3.10-clang12,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-py3-gcc11-inductor-benchmarks,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.12-halide,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.12-pallas,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.12-pallas,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-xpu-n-1-py3,\n          pytorch-linux-noble-xpu-n-py3,\n          pytorch-linux-noble-xpu-n-py3-inductor-benchmarks,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-py3-clang18-asan,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-py3-clang12-onnx,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-linter,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3.10-linter,\n          # TODO: Re-enable me when docker pin update happens\n          # pytorch-linux-jammy-py3-clang12-executorch,\n          pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.12-triton-cpu,\n          pytorch-linux-noble-riscv64-py3.12-gcc14\n        ]\n        include:\n          - docker-image-name: pytorch-linux-jammy-aarch64-py3.10-gcc13\n            runner: linux.arm64.m7g.4xlarge\n          - docker-image-name: pytorch-linux-jammy-aarch64-py3.10-clang21\n            runner: linux.arm64.m7g.4xlarge\n          - docker-image-name: pytorch-linux-jammy-aarch64-py3.10-gcc13-inductor-benchmarks\n            runner: linux.arm64.m7g.4xlarge\n            timeout-minutes: 600\n    # Docker uploads fail from LF runners, see https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/137358\n    # runs-on: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}${{ matrix.runner }}\"\n    runs-on: \"${{ matrix.runner }}\"",
    "filePath": "/workspace/.github/workflows/docker-builds.yml",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 45,
    "totalLines": 169
  },
  "type": "text"
}
151. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/periodic.yml"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "name: periodic\n\non:\n  schedule:\n    # We have several schedules so jobs can check github.event.schedule to activate only for a fraction of the runs.\n    # Also run less frequently on weekends.\n    - cron: 45 0,8,16 * * 1-5\n    - cron: 45 4 * * 0,6\n    - cron: 45 4,12,20 * * 1-5\n    - cron: 45 12 * * 0,6\n    - cron: 29 8 * * *  # about 1:29am PDT, for mem leak check and rerun disabled tests\n  push:\n    tags:\n      - ciflow/periodic/*\n    branches:\n      - release/*\n  workflow_dispatch:\n\nconcurrency:\n  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}-${{ github.event.schedule }}\n  cancel-in-progress: true\n\npermissions:\n  id-token: write\n  contents: read\n\njobs:\n  llm-td:\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    name: before-test\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/llm_td_retrieval.yml\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n\n  target-determination:\n    name: before-test\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/target_determination.yml\n    needs: llm-td\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n\n  get-label-type:\n    name: get-label-type\n    uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n    if: (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    with:\n      triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\n      issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\n      curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\n      curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\n\n  linux-jammy-cuda12_4-py3_10-gcc11-build:\n    name: linux-jammy-cuda12.4-py3.10-gcc11\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.4-py3.10-gcc11\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.4-cudnn9-py3-gcc11\n      cuda-arch-list: 7.5\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"legacy_nvidia_driver\", shard: 1, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g4dn.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu\" },\n          { config: \"legacy_nvidia_driver\", shard: 2, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g4dn.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu\" },\n          { config: \"legacy_nvidia_driver\", shard: 3, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g4dn.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu\" },\n          { config: \"legacy_nvidia_driver\", shard: 4, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g4dn.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu\" },\n          { config: \"legacy_nvidia_driver\", shard: 5, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g4dn.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu\" },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-cuda12_4-py3_10-gcc11-test:\n    name: }}linux.g5.12xlarge.nvidia.gpu\", owners: [\"oncall:distributed\"] },\n          { config: \"multigpu\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type 152. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "grep -B5 -A5 \"opt_out_experiments\" /workspace/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml | head -30",
  "description": "View opt_out_experiments in runner determinator"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "      check_experiments:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: |\n          List of experiments for this workflow. If not defined, all default experiments are included.\n      opt_out_experiments:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: Comma-separated list of experiments this workflow will opt-out of.\n      triggering_actor:\n        required: true\n--\n      GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n      ISSUE_NUMBER: ${{ inputs.issue_number }}\n      TRIGGERING_ACTOR: ${{ inputs.triggering_actor }}\n      ISSUE_OWNER: ${{ inputs.issue_owner }}\n      CHECK_EXPERIMENTS: ${{ inputs.check_experiments }}\n      OPT_OUT_EXPERIMENTS: ${{ inputs.opt_out_experiments }}\n      PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n    steps:\n      # - name: Checkout PyTorch\n      #   uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n      #   with:\n--\n          def get_runner_prefix(\n              rollout_state: str,\n              workflow_requestors: Iterable[str],\n              branch: str,\n              eligible_experiments: frozenset[str] = frozenset(),\n              opt_out_experiments: frozenset[str] = frozenset(),"
}
