155. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "head -80 /workspace/.github/templates/macos_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2",
  "description": "View macOS template"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "{% import 'common.yml.j2' as common %}\n{% import 'upload.yml.j2' as upload %}\n\n{%- block name -%}\n# Template is at:    .github/templates/macos_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2\n# Generation script: .github/scripts/generate_ci_workflows.py\nname: !{{ build_environment }}\n{%- endblock %}\n\n{%- macro set_runner_specific_vars() -%}\n      # NOTE: These environment variables are put here so that they can be applied on every job equally\n      #       They are also here because setting them at a workflow level doesn't give us access to the\n      #       runner.temp variable, which we need.\n      - name: Populate binary env\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          # shellcheck disable=SC2129\n          echo \"BINARY_ENV_FILE=${RUNNER_TEMP}/env\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n          # shellcheck disable=SC2129\n          echo \"PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR=${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n          # shellcheck disable=SC2129\n          echo \"MAC_PACKAGE_WORK_DIR=${RUNNER_TEMP}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n{%- endmacro %}\n\n{%- macro setup_python(py_ver) -%}\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v6\n        with:\n          # .4 version is min minor for 3.10, and also no-gil version of 3.13 needs at least 3.13.3\n          python-version: \"!{{ py_ver.strip('t') + ('.4' if '3.14' not in py_ver else '.0') }}\"\n          freethreaded: !{{ \"true\" if py_ver.endswith('t') else \"false\" }}\n{%- endmacro %}\n\non:\n# TODO: Migrate to new ciflow trigger, reference https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/70321\n  push:\n    # NOTE: Meta Employees can trigger new nightlies using: https://fburl.com/trigger_pytorch_nightly_build\n    branches:\n      - nightly\n    tags:\n      # NOTE: Binary build pipelines should only get triggered on release candidate builds\n      # Release candidate tags look like: v1.11.0-rc1\n      - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+-rc[0-9]+\n{%- for label in ciflow_config.labels | sort %}\n    {%- if loop.first and branches != \"nightly\" %}\n    tags:\n    {%- endif %}\n      - '!{{ label }}/*'\n{%- endfor %}\n  workflow_dispatch:\n\nenv:\n  ALPINE_IMAGE: \"308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine\"\n  AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1\n  BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: !{{ build_environment }}\n  GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n  PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n  SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 0\n!{{ common.concurrency(build_environment) }}\n\njobs:\n{%- for config in build_configs %}\n  !{{ config[\"build_name\"] }}-build:\n    if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    runs-on: !{{ macos_runner }}\n    timeout-minutes: !{{ common.timeout_minutes }}\n    !{{ upload.binary_env(config, true) }}\n    {%- if config.pytorch_extra_install_requirements is defined and config.pytorch_extra_install_requirements|d('')|length > 0  %}\n      PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS: !{{ config.pytorch_extra_install_requirements }}\n    {%- endif %}\n    steps:\n      !{{ set_runner_specific_vars() }}\n      !{{ setup_python(config.get(\"python_version\", \"3.10\")) }}\n      !{{ common.checkout(deep_clone=False, directory=\"pytorch\", checkout_pr_head=False) }}\n      - name: Populate binary env\n        run: |\n          \"${PYTORCH_ROOT}/.circleci/scripts/binary_populate_env.sh\"\n      - name: Build PyTorch binary\n        run: |\n          set -eux -o pipefail"
}

{
  "file": {
    "content": "name: inductor-rocm-mi300\n\non:\n  push:\n    branches:\n      - main\n      - release/*\n    tags:\n      - ciflow/inductor-rocm/*\n      - ciflow/inductor-rocm-mi300/*\n  workflow_dispatch:\n\nconcurrency:\n  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\n  cancel-in-progress: true\n\npermissions:\n  id-token: write\n  contents: read\n\njobs:\n  target-determination:\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    name: before-test\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/target_determination.yml\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n\n  get-label-type:\n    name: get-label-type\n    uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n    if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    with:\n      triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\n      issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\n      curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\n      curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\n      opt_out_experiments: lf\n\n  linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-inductor-build:\n    name: rocm-py3.12-inductor-mi300\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-noble-rocm-py3.12-mi300\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-noble-rocm-n-py3\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"inductor\", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: \"linux.rocm.gpu.gfx942.1\" },\n          { config: \"inductor\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \"linux.rocm.gpu.gfx942.1\" },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-inductor-test:\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n    name: rocm-py3.12-inductor-mi300\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_rocm-test.yml\n    needs: linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-inductor-build\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-inductor-build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-inductor-build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix:  ${{ needs.linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-inductor-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n    secrets: inherit\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/.github/workflows/inductor-rocm-mi300.yml",

    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 68

180. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/build-libtorch-images.yml"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "name: Build libtorch docker images\n\non:\n  push:\n    branches:\n      - main\n      - release/*\n    tags:\n      # NOTE: Binary build pipelines should only get triggered on release candidate or nightly builds\n      # Release candidate tags look like: v1.11.0-rc1\n      - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+-rc[0-9]+\n    paths:\n      - .ci/docker/**\n      - .github/workflows/build-libtorch-images.yml\n      - .github/actions/binary-docker-build/**\n  pull_request:\n    paths:\n      - .ci/docker/**\n      - .github/workflows/build-libtorch-images.yml\n      - .github/actions/binary-docker-build/**\n\nenv:\n  DOCKER_REGISTRY: \"docker.io\"\n  DOCKER_BUILDKIT: 1\n  WITH_PUSH: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release') || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) }}\n\nconcurrency:\n  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\n  cancel-in-progress: true\n\njobs:\n  get-label-type:\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    name: get-label-type\n    uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n    with:\n      triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\n      issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\n      curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\n      curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\n\n  build:\n    environment: ${{ (github.event_name == 'push' && (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release') || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) && 'docker-build') || '' }}\n    needs: get-label-type\n    runs-on: ${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.9xlarge.ephemeral\n    name: libtorch-cxx11-builder:${{ matrix.tag }}\n    strategy:\n      fail-fast: false\n      matrix:\n        include: [\n          { tag: \"cuda13.0\" },\n          { tag: \"cuda12.9\" },\n          { tag: \"cuda12.8\" },\n          { tag: \"cuda12.6\" },\n          { tag: \"rocm7.0\"  },\n          { tag: \"rocm7.1\"  },\n          { tag: \"cpu\"      },\n        ]\n    steps:\n      - name: Build docker image\n        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/binary-docker-build@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image-name: libtorch-cxx11-builder\n          custom-tag-prefix: ${{ matrix.tag }}\n          docker-build-dir: libtorch\n          DOCKER_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_TOKEN }}\n          DOCKER_ID: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_ID }}\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/.github/workflows/build-libtorch-images.yml",
    "numLines": 68,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 68
  },
  "type": "text"
}

ci-image:pytorch-linux-noble-rocm-n-py3\n/workspace/.github/workflows/operator_microbenchmark.yml:98:      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-rocm-n-py3-benchmarks\n/workspace/.github/workflows/periodic-rocm-mi200.yml:61:      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-rocm-n-py3\n/workspace/.github/workflows/periodic-rocm-mi300.yml:60:      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-noble-rocm-n-py3\n/workspace/.github/workflows/pull.yml:315:      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-rocm-n-py3\n/workspace/.github/workflows/rocm-mi200.yml:48:      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-rocm-n-py3\n/workspace/.github/workflows/rocm-mi300.yml:48:      docker-image-name: 182. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.ci/docker/ubuntu-rocm/Dockerfile"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "ARG UBUNTU_VERSION\n\nFROM ubuntu:${UBUNTU_VERSION}\n\nARG UBUNTU_VERSION\n\nENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND noninteractive\n\n# Set AMD gpu targets to build for\nARG PYTORCH_ROCM_ARCH\nENV PYTORCH_ROCM_ARCH ${PYTORCH_ROCM_ARCH}\n\n# Install common dependencies (so that this step can be cached separately)\nCOPY ./common/install_base.sh install_base.sh\nRUN bash ./install_base.sh && rm install_base.sh\n\n# Install user\nCOPY ./common/install_user.sh install_user.sh\nRUN bash ./install_user.sh && rm install_user.sh\n\n# Install katex\nARG KATEX\nCOPY ./common/install_docs_reqs.sh install_docs_reqs.sh\nRUN bash ./install_docs_reqs.sh && rm install_docs_reqs.sh\n\n# Install conda and other packages (e.g., numpy, pytest)\nARG ANACONDA_PYTHON_VERSION\nARG BUILD_ENVIRONMENT\nENV ANACONDA_PYTHON_VERSION=$ANACONDA_PYTHON_VERSION\nENV PATH /opt/conda/envs/py_$ANACONDA_PYTHON_VERSION/bin:/opt/conda/bin:$PATH\nCOPY requirements-ci.txt /opt/conda/requirements-ci.txt\nCOPY ./common/install_conda.sh install_conda.sh\nCOPY ./common/common_utils.sh common_utils.sh\nRUN bash ./install_conda.sh && rm install_conda.sh common_utils.sh /opt/conda/requirements-ci.txt\n\n# Install gcc\nARG GCC_VERSION\nCOPY ./common/install_gcc.sh install_gcc.sh\nRUN bash ./install_gcc.sh && rm install_gcc.sh\n\n# Install clang\nARG CLANG_VERSION\nCOPY ./common/install_clang.sh install_clang.sh\nRUN bash ./install_clang.sh && rm install_clang.sh\n\n# (optional) Install vision packages like OpenCV\nARG VISION\nCOPY ./common/install_vision.sh ./common/cache_vision_models.sh ./common/common_utils.sh ./\nRUN if [ -n \"${VISION}\" ]; then bash ./install_vision.sh; fi\nRUN rm install_vision.sh cache_vision_models.sh common_utils.sh\nENV INSTALLED_VISION ${VISION}\n\n# Install rocm\nARG ROCM_VERSION\nRUN mkdir ci_commit_pins\nCOPY ./common/common_utils.sh common_utils.sh\nCOPY ./ci_commit_pins/rocm-composable-kernel.txt ci_commit_pins/rocm-composable-kernel.txt\nCOPY ./common/install_rocm.sh install_rocm.sh\nRUN bash ./install_rocm.sh\nRUN rm install_rocm.sh common_utils.sh\nRUN rm -r ci_commit_pins\nCOPY ./common/install_rocm_magma.sh install_rocm_magma.sh\nRUN bash ./install_rocm_magma.sh ${ROCM_VERSION}\nRUN rm install_rocm_magma.sh\nADD ./common/install_miopen.sh install_miopen.sh\nRUN bash ./install_miopen.sh ${ROCM_VERSION} && rm install_miopen.sh\nENV ROCM_PATH /opt/rocm\nENV PATH /opt/rocm/bin:$PATH\nENV PATH /opt/rocm/hcc/bin:$PATH\nENV PATH /opt/rocm/hip/bin:$PATH\nENV PATH /opt/rocm/opencl/bin:$PATH\nENV PATH /opt/rocm/llvm/bin:$PATH\nENV MAGMA_HOME /opt/rocm/magma\nENV LANG C.UTF-8\nENV LC_ALL C.UTF-8\n\n# Install amdsmi\nCOPY ./common/install_amdsmi.sh install_amdsmi.sh\nRUN bash ./install_amdsmi.sh\nRUN rm install_amdsmi.sh\n\n# (optional) Install UCC\nARG UCX_COMMIT\nARG UCC_COMMIT\nENV UCX_COMMIT $UCX_COMMIT\nENV UCC_COMMIT $UCC_COMMIT\nENV UCX_HOME /usr\nENV UCC_HOME /usr\nADD ./common/install_ucc.sh install_ucc.sh\nRUN if [ -n \"${UCX_COMMIT}\" ] && [ -n \"${UCC_COMMIT}\" ]; then bash ./install_ucc.sh; fi\nRUN rm install_ucc.sh\n\nCOPY ./common/install_openssl.sh install_openssl.sh\nENV OPENSSL_ROOT_DIR /opt/openssl\nRUN bash ./install_openssl.sh\nENV OPENSSL_DIR /opt/openssl\n\nARG INDUCTOR_BENCHMARKS\nARG ANACONDA_PYTHON_VERSION\nENV ANACONDA_PYTHON_VERSION=$ANACONDA_PYTHON_VERSION\nCOPY ./common/install_inductor_benchmark_deps.sh install_inductor_benchmark_deps.sh\nCOPY ./common/common_utils.sh common_utils.sh\nCOPY ci_commit_pins/huggingface-requirements.txt huggingface-requirements.txt\nCOPY ci_commit_pins/timm.txt timm.txt\nCOPY ci_commit_pins/torchbench.txt torchbench.txt\nRUN if [ -n \"${INDUCTOR_BENCHMARKS}\" ]; then bash ./install_inductor_benchmark_deps.sh; fi\nRUN rm install_inductor_benchmark_deps.sh common_utils.sh timm.txt huggingface-requirements.txt torchbench.txt\n\n# (optional) Install non-default Ninja version\nARG NINJA_VERSION\nCOPY ./common/install_ninja.sh install_ninja.sh\nRUN if [ -n \"${NINJA_VERSION}\" ]; then bash ./install_ninja.sh; fi\nRUN rm install_ninja.sh\n\nARG TRITON\n# Install triton, this needs to be done before sccache because the latter will\n# try to reach out to S3, which docker build runners don't have access\nCOPY ./common/install_triton.sh install_triton.sh\nCOPY ./common/common_utils.sh common_utils.sh\nCOPY ci_commit_pins/triton.txt triton.txt\nCOPY triton_version.txt triton_version.txt\nRUN if [ -n \"${TRITON}\" ]; then bash ./install_triton.sh; fi\nRUN rm install_triton.sh common_utils.sh triton.txt triton_version.txt\n\n\n# Install ccache/sccache (do this last, so we get priority in PATH)\nCOPY ./common/install_cache.sh install_cache.sh\nENV PATH /opt/cache/bin:$PATH\nRUN bash ./install_cache.sh && rm install_cache.sh\n\n# Install Open MPI for ROCm\nCOPY ./common/install_openmpi.sh install_openmpi.sh\nRUN if [ -n \"${CUDA_VERSION}\" ]; then bash install_openmpi.sh; fi\nRUN rm install_openmpi.sh\n\n# Include BUILD_ENVIRONMENT environment variable in image\nARG BUILD_ENVIRONMENT\nENV BUILD_ENVIRONMENT ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT}\n\n# Install LLVM dev version (Defined in the pytorch/builder github repository)\nCOPY --from=pytorch/llvm:9.0.1 /opt/llvm /opt/llvm\n\nUSER jenkins\nCMD [\"bash\"]\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/.ci/docker/ubuntu-rocm/Dockerfile",
    "numLines": 145,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 145
  },
  "type": "text"
}
183. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "grep -n \"ROCM_VERSION\\|7\\.0\\|7\\.1\\|7\\.2\\|6\\.\" /workspace/.ci/docker/common/install_rocm.sh | head -20",
  "description": "Find ROCm version in install script"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "36:    # we want the patch version of 6.4 instead\n37:    if [[ $(ver $ROCM_VERSION) -eq $(ver 6.4) ]]; then\n38:        ROCM_VERSION=\"${ROCM_VERSION}.2\"\n42:    rocm_baseurl=\"http://repo.radeon.com/rocm/apt/${ROCM_VERSION}\"\n59:    if [[ $(ver $ROCM_VERSION) -ge $(ver 6.1) ]]; then\n63:    if [[ $(ver $ROCM_VERSION) -lt $(ver 7.1) ]]; then\n64:      # precompiled miopen kernels added in ROCm 3.5, renamed in ROCm 5.5, removed in ROCm 7.1\n75:    # ROCm 6.0 had a regression where journal_mode was enabled on the kdb files resulting in permission errors at runtime\n81:    # ROCm 6.3 had a regression where initializing static code objects had significant overhead\n82:    # CI no longer builds for ROCm 6.3, but\n83:    # ROCm 6.4 did not yet fix the regression, also HIP branch names are different\n84:    if [[ $(ver $ROCM_VERSION) -ge $(ver 6.4) ]] && [[ $(ver $ROCM_VERSION) -lt $(ver 7.0) ]]; then\n85:        if [[ $(ver $ROCM_VERSION) -eq $(ver 6.4.2) ]]; then\n86:            HIP_TAG=rocm-6.4.2\n87:            CLR_HASH=74d78ba3ac4bac235d02bcb48511c30b5cfdd457  # branch release/rocm-rel-6.4.2-statco-hotfix\n88:        elif [[ $(ver $ROCM_VERSION) -eq $(ver 6.4.1) ]]; then\n89:            HIP_TAG=rocm-6.4.1\n90:            CLR_HASH=efe6c35790b9206923bfeed1209902feff37f386  # branch release/rocm-rel-6.4.1-statco-hotfix\n91:        elif [[ $(ver $ROCM_VERSION) -eq $(ver 6.4) ]]; then\n92:            HIP_TAG=rocm-6.4.0"
}

TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\n255:       #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\n256:       DESIRED_CUDA: cu129\n257:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION: \"12.9\"\n258:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE: cuda\n259:       DOCKER_IMAGE: manylinux2_28-builder\n260:       DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX: cuda12.9\n261:       DESIRED_PYTHON: \"3.10\"\n262:       runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n263:       build_name: manywheel-py3_10-cuda12_9\n264:       build_environment: linux-binary-manywheel\n265:       PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS: cuda-bindings==12.9.4; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-cuda-nvrtc-cu12==12.9.86; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-cuda-runtime-cu12==12.9.79; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-cuda-cupti-cu12==12.9.79; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-cudnn-cu12==9.10.2.21; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-cublas-cu12==12.9.1.4; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-cufft-cu12==11.4.1.4; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-curand-cu12==10.3.10.19; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-cusolver-cu12==11.7.5.82; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-cusparse-cu12==12.5.10.65; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-cusparselt-cu12==0.7.1; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-nccl-cu12==2.27.5; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-nvshmem-cu12==3.4.5; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-nvtx-cu12==12.9.79; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-nvjitlink-cu12==12.9.86; platform_system == 'Linux' | nvidia-cufile-cu12==1.14.1.1; platform_system == 'Linux'\n266:     secrets:\n267:       github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n268: \n269:   manywheel-py3_10-cuda12_9-test:  # Testing\n270:     if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n271:     needs:\n272:       - manywheel-py3_10-cuda12_9-build\n273:       - get-label-type\n274:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_binary-test-linux.yml\n275:     with:\n276:       PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch\n277:       PACKAGE_TYPE: manywheel\n278:       # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\n279:       #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\n280:       DESIRED_CUDA: cu129\n281:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION: \"12.9\"\n282:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE: cuda\n283:       DOCKER_IMAGE: manylinux2_28-builder\n284:       DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX: cuda12.9\n285:       DESIRED_PYTHON: \"3.10\"\n286:       build_name: manywheel-py3_10-cuda12_9\n287:       build_environment: linux-binary-manywheel\n288:       runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n289:       runs_on: linux.g4dn.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu # 12.8+ builds need sm_70+ runner\n290:     secrets:\n291:       github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n292:   manywheel-py3_10-cuda12_9-upload:  # Uploading\n293:     if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n294:     permissions:\n295:       id-token: write\n296:       contents: read\n297:     needs: manywheel-py3_10-cuda12_9-test\n298:     with:\n299:       PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch\n300:       PACKAGE_TYPE: manywheel\n301:       # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\n302:       #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\n303:       DESIRED_CUDA: cu129\n304:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION: \"12.9\"\n305:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE: cuda\n306:       DOCKER_IMAGE: manylinux2_28-builder\n307:       DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX: cuda12.9\n308:       DESIRED_PYTHON: \"3.10\"\n309:       build_name: manywheel-py3_10-cuda12_9\n310:     secrets:\n311:       github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n312:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_binary-upload.yml\n313:"
}

191. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/actions/binary-docker-build/action.yml"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "name: Binary docker build\n\ndescription: Build docker image for binary builds\n\ninputs:\n  docker-image-name:\n    description: Docker image name for PR builds\n    required: true\n  docker-build-dir:\n    description: Location of the build.sh relative to .ci/docker\n    required: true\n  custom-tag-prefix:\n    description: Custom tag prefix for the docker image\n    required: false\n  DOCKER_TOKEN:\n    description: Docker token for authentication\n    required: true\n  DOCKER_ID:\n    description: Docker ID for authentication\n    required: true\n\nruns:\n  using: composite\n  steps:\n    - name: Checkout PyTorch\n      uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main\n\n    - name: Calculate docker image\n      id: calculate-docker-image\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main\n      with:\n        docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}\n        docker-build-dir: .ci/docker\n        custom-tag-prefix: ${{ inputs.custom-tag-prefix }}\n        docker-build-script: ${{ inputs.docker-build-dir }}/build.sh\n        always-rebuild: true\n        push: true\n\n    - name: Tag and (if WITH_PUSH) push docker image to docker.io\n      env:\n        DOCKER_TOKEN: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_TOKEN }}\n        DOCKER_ID: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_ID }}\n        DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}\n        DOCKER_IMAGE_PREFIX: ${{ inputs.custom-tag-prefix }}\n        CREATED_FULL_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        set -euox pipefail\n        GITHUB_REF=${GITHUB_REF:-$(git symbolic-ref -q HEAD || git describe --tags --exact-match)}\n        GIT_BRANCH_NAME=${GITHUB_REF##*/}\n        GIT_COMMIT_SHA=${GITHUB_SHA:-$(git rev-parse HEAD)}\n        CI_FOLDER_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD:.ci/docker)\n\n        DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX=docker.io/pytorch/${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME}:${DOCKER_IMAGE_PREFIX}\n\n        docker tag ${CREATED_FULL_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME} ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}\n        docker tag ${CREATED_FULL_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME} ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${GIT_BRANCH_NAME}\n        docker tag ${CREATED_FULL_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME} ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${GIT_COMMIT_SHA}\n        docker tag ${CREATED_FULL_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME} ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${CI_FOLDER_SHA}\n\n        # Pretty sure Github will mask tokens and I'm not sure if it will even be\n        # printed due to pipe, but just in case\n        set +x\n        if [[ ${WITH_PUSH:-false} == \"true\" ]]; then\n          echo \"${DOCKER_TOKEN}\" | docker login -u \"${DOCKER_ID}\" --password-stdin\n          docker push ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}\n          docker push ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${GIT_BRANCH_NAME}\n          docker push ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${GIT_COMMIT_SHA}\n          docker push ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${CI_FOLDER_SHA}\n        fi\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/.github/actions/binary-docker-build/action.yml",
    "numLines": 71,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 71
  },
  "type": "text"
}
192. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/actions/linux-test/action.yml"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "name: linux-test\n\ninputs:\n  build-environment:\n    required: true\n    type: string\n    description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\n  test-matrix:\n    required: true\n    type: string\n    description: JSON description of what test configs to run.\n  docker-image:\n    required: true\n    type: string\n    description: Docker image to run in.\n  sync-tag:\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"\"\n    description: |\n      If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\n      job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\n  use-gha:\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"\"\n    description: If set to any value, upload to GHA. Otherwise upload to S3.\n  dashboard-tag:\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"\"\n  s3-bucket:\n    description: S3 bucket to download artifact\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"gha-artifacts\"\n  aws-role-to-assume:\n    description: role to assume for downloading artifacts\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"\"\n  HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\n    description: |\n      HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\n    required: false\n    default: \"\"\n  GITHUB_TOKEN:\n    description: GitHub token\n    required: true\n  disable-monitor:\n    description: |\n      [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.\n      Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,\n      since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.\n    required: false\n    type: boolean\n    default: true\n#env:\n#  GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ inputs.default_branch }}\n\nruns:\n  using: composite\n  steps:\n    - name: Setup Linux\n      uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\n\n    - name: configure aws credentials\n      if : ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' }}\n      uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4\n      with:\n        role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}\n        role-session-name: gha-linux-test\n        aws-region: us-east-1\n\n    - name: Calculate docker image\n      id: calculate-docker-image\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main\n      with:\n        docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n\n    - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image\n      id: print-ghcr-mirror\n      env:\n        ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*/}\n        echo \"docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}\"\n\n    - name: Pull docker image\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main\n      with:\n        docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n\n    - name: Check if in a container runner\n      shell: bash\n      id: check_container_runner\n      run: echo \"IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER=$(if [ -f /.inarc ] || [ -f /.incontainer ]; then echo true ; else echo false; fi)\" >> \"$GITHUB_OUTPUT\"\n\n    - name: Install nvidia driver, nvidia-docker runtime, set GPU_FLAG\n      id: install-nvidia-driver\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@main\n      if: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'cuda') && !contains(matrix.config, 'nogpu') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false' }}\n\n    - name: Setup GPU_FLAG for docker run\n      id: setup-gpu-flag\n      run: echo \"GPU_FLAG=--gpus all -e NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES=all\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n      if: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'cuda') && !contains(matrix.config, 'nogpu') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' }}\n\n    - name: Setup SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT environment for docker run when on container\n      id: setup-sscache-port-flag\n      run: echo \"SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG=-e SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT=$((RUNNER_UID + 4226))\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n      if: ${{ steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' }}\n\n    - name: Lock NVIDIA A100 40GB Frequency\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        sudo nvidia-smi -pm 1\n        sudo nvidia-smi -ac 1215,1410\n        nvidia-smi\n      if: ${{ contains(matrix.runner, 'a100') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false' }}\n\n    - name: Start monitoring script\n      id: monitor-script\n      if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\n      shell: bash\n      continue-on-error: true\n      run: |\n        python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 nvidia-ml-py==11.525.84\n        python3 -m tools.stats.monitor > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &\n        echo \"monitor-script-pid=${!}\" >> \"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\"\n\n    - name: Download build artifacts\n      uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts\n      with:\n        name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n        s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\n\n    - name: Download TD artifacts\n      continue-on-error: true\n      uses: ./.github/actions/download-td-artifacts\n\n    - name: Parse ref\n      id: parse-ref\n      shell: bash\n      run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\n\n    - name: Get workflow job id\n      id: get-job-id\n      uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\n      if: always()\n      with:\n        github-token: ${{ inputs.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n    - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues\n      # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently\n      # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do\n      # any filtering.  All filtering is done in the build step.\n      id: keep-going\n      uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\n      with:\n        github-token: ${{ inputs.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\n        job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n\n    - name: Test\n      id: test\n      env:\n        BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n        PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n        GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\n        GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\n        GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\n        GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\n        GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\n        GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\n        JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n        JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n        BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\n        SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n        BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha || github.sha }}\n        TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}\n        SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}\n        NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}\n        REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.reenabled-issues }}\n        CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}\n        VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}\n        TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}\n        NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}\n        NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}\n        TD_DISTRIBUTED: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-td-distributed }}\n        SCCACHE_BUCKET: ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2\n        SCCACHE_REGION: us-east-1\n        SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX: ${{ github.workflow }}\n        SHM_SIZE: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'cuda') && '2g' || '1g' }}\n        DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n        XLA_CUDA: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'xla') && '0' || '' }}\n        XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla\n        PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}\n        PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}\n        DASHBOARD_TAG: ${{ inputs.dashboard-tag }}\n        HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ inputs.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}\n        SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}\n        IS_A100_RUNNER: ${{ contains(matrix.runner, 'a100') && '1' || '0' }}\n\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        set -x\n\n        if [[ $TEST_CONFIG == 'multigpu' ]]; then\n          TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/multigpu-test.sh\n        elif [[ $BUILD_ENVIRONMENT == *onnx* ]]; then\n          TEST_COMMAND=.ci/onnx/test.sh\n        else\n          TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test.sh\n        fi\n\n        # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\n        # TODO: Stop building test binaries as part of the build phase\n        # Used for GPU_FLAG since that doesn't play nice\n        # shellcheck disable=SC2086,SC2090\n        container_name=$(docker run \\\n          ${GPU_FLAG:-} \\\n          ${SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG:-} \\\n          -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\n          -e PR_NUMBER \\\n          -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \\\n          -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \\\n          -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \\\n          -e GITHUB_JOB \\\n          -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \\\n          -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \\\n          -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \\\n          -e JOB_ID \\\n          -e JOB_NAME \\\n          -e BASE_SHA \\\n          -e BRANCH \\\n          -e SHA1 \\\n          -e AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \\\n          -e IN_WHEEL_TEST \\\n          -e SHARD_NUMBER \\\n          -e TEST_CONFIG \\\n          -e NUM_TEST_SHARDS \\\n          -e REENABLED_ISSUES \\\n          -e CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR \\\n          -e VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS \\\n          -e NO_TEST_TIMEOUT \\\n          -e NO_TD \\\n          -e TD_DISTRIBUTED \\\n          -e PR_LABELS \\\n          -e MAX_JOBS=\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\" \\\n          -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \\\n          -e SCCACHE_REGION \\\n          -e SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX \\\n          -e XLA_CUDA \\\n          -e XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME \\\n          -e PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK \\\n          -e PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS \\\n          -e SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1 \\\n          -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \\\n          -e SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN \\\n          -e DASHBOARD_TAG \\\n          -e IS_A100_RUNNER \\\n          --env-file=\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\" \\\n          --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\n          --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\n          --ipc=host \\\n          --shm-size=\"${SHM_SIZE}\" \\\n          --tty \\\n          --detach \\\n          --name=\"${container_name}\" \\\n          --user jenkins \\\n          -v \"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\" \\\n          -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\n          \"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\"\n        )\n        echo \"DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID=${container_name}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n        docker exec -t \"${container_name}\" sh -c \"pip install $(echo dist/*.whl)[opt-einsum] && ${TEST_COMMAND}\"\n\n    - name: Upload pytest cache if tests failed\n      uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-upload\n      continue-on-error: true\n      if: failure() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion == 'failure'\n      with:\n        cache_dir: .pytest_cache\n        shard: ${{ matrix.shard }}\n        sha: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n        test_config: ${{ matrix.config }}\n        job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n\n    - name: Print remaining test logs\n      shell: bash\n      if: always() && steps.test.conclusion\n      run: |\n        cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true\n\n    - name: Stop monitoring script\n      if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n      shell: bash\n      continue-on-error: true\n      env:\n        MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n      run: |\n        kill \"$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID\"\n\n    - name: Upload test artifacts\n      uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts\n      if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'\n      with:\n        file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n        use-gha: ${{ inputs.use-gha }}\n        s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\n\n    - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        # shellcheck disable=SC2156\n        find . -iname \"core.[1-9]*\" -exec docker exec \"${DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID}\" sh -c \"gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'\" \\;\n\n    - name: Store Core dumps on S3\n      uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@v5\n      if: failure()\n      with:\n        name: coredumps-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}\n        retention-days: 14\n        if-no-files-found: ignore\n        path: ./**/core.[1-9]*\n\n    - name: Teardown Linux\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main\n      if: always() && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false'\n\n    # NB: We are currently having an intermittent GPU-related issue on G5 runners with\n    # A10G GPU. Once this happens, trying to reset the GPU as done in setup-nvidia does\n    # not seem to help. Here are some symptoms:\n    #   * Calling nvidia-smi timeouts after 60 second\n    #   * Fail to run nvidia-smi with an unable to determine the device handle for GPU\n    #     unknown error\n    #   * Test fails with a missing CUDA GPU error when initializing CUDA in PyTorch\n    #   * Run docker --gpus all fails with error response from daemon\n    #\n    # As both the root cause and recovery path are unclear, let's take the runner out of\n    # service so that it doesn't get any more jobs\n    - name: Check NVIDIA driver installation step\n      if: failure() && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outcome && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outcome != 'skipped'\n      shell: bash\n      env:\n        RUNNER_WORKSPACE: ${{ runner.workspace }}\n      run: |\n        set +e\n        set -x\n\n        nvidia-smi\n        # NB: Surprisingly, nvidia-smi command returns successfully with return code 0 even in\n        # the case where the driver has already crashed as it still can get the driver version\n        # and some basic information like the bus ID.  However, the rest of the information\n        # would be missing (ERR!), for example:\n        #\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        # | NVIDIA-SMI 525.89.02    Driver Version: 525.89.02    CUDA Version: 12.0     |\n        # |-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n        # | GPU  Name        Persistence-M| Bus-Id        Disp.A | Volatile Uncorr. ECC |\n        # | Fan  Temp  Perf  Pwr:Usage/Cap|         Memory-Usage | GPU-Util  Compute M. |\n        # |                               |                      |               MIG M. |\n        # |===============================+======================+======================|\n        # |   0  ERR!                Off  | 00000000:00:1E.0 Off |                 ERR! |\n        # |ERR!  ERR! ERR!    ERR! / ERR! |   4184MiB / 23028MiB |    ERR!      Default |\n        # |                               |                      |                 ERR! |\n        # +-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n        #\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        # | Processes:                                                                  |\n        # |  GPU   GI   CI        PID   Type   Process name                  GPU Memory |\n        # |        ID   ID                                                   Usage      |\n        # |=============================================================================|\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        #\n        # This should be reported as a failure instead as it will guarantee to fail when\n        # Docker tries to run with --gpus all\n        #\n        # So, the correct check here is to query one of the missing piece of info like\n        # GPU name, so that the command can fail accordingly\n        nvidia-smi --query-gpu=gpu_name --format=csv,noheader --id=0\n        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n\n        # For runner with multiple GPUs, we also want to confirm that the number of GPUs are the\n        # power of 2, i.e. 1, 2, 4, or 8. This is to avoid flaky test issue when one GPU fails\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/4000\n        GPU_COUNT=$(nvidia-smi --list-gpus | wc -l)\n        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n\n        # Check the GPU count to be a power of 2\n        if [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -le 8 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 1 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 2 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 4 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 8 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver detects $GPU_COUNT GPUs. The runner has a broken GPU, shutting it down...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/.github/actions/linux-test/action.yml",
    "numLines": 410,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 410
  },
  "type": "text"
}
193. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/actions/setup-rocm/action.yml"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "name: Setup ROCm host\n\ndescription: Set up ROCm host for CI\n\nruns:\n  using: composite\n  steps:\n    - name: Runner ROCm version\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        dpkg -l | grep -E \"  rocm\"\n\n    - name: Stop all running docker containers\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        # ignore expansion of \"docker ps -q\" since it could be empty\n        # shellcheck disable=SC2046\n        docker stop $(docker ps -q) || true\n        # Prune all stopped containers.\n        docker container prune -f\n\n    - name: Runner health check system info\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        cat /etc/os-release || true\n        cat /etc/apt/sources.list.d/rocm.list || true\n        cat /opt/rocm/.info/version || true\n        whoami\n\n    - name: Runner health check amdgpu info\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        dpkg -l | grep -E \"  amdgpu\"\n\n    - name: Runner health check rocm-smi\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        rocm-smi\n\n    - name: Runner health check rocminfo\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        rocminfo\n\n    - name: Runner health check GPU count\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        ngpu=$(rocminfo | grep -c -E 'Name:.*\\sgfx')\n        msg=\"Please file an issue on pytorch/pytorch reporting the faulty runner. Include a link to the runner logs so the runner can be identified\"\n        if [[ $ngpu -eq 0 ]]; then\n            echo \"Error: Failed to detect any GPUs on the runner\"\n            echo \"$msg\"\n            exit 1\n        fi\n\n    - name: Runner diskspace health check\n      uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/diskspace-cleanup@main\n      if: always()\n\n    - name: Runner health check disconnect on failure\n      if: ${{ failure() }}\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        killall runsvc.sh\n\n    - name: Setup useful environment variables\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        RUNNER_ARTIFACT_DIR=\"${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts\"\n        rm -rf \"${RUNNER_ARTIFACT_DIR}\"\n        mkdir -p \"${RUNNER_ARTIFACT_DIR}\"\n        echo \"RUNNER_ARTIFACT_DIR=${RUNNER_ARTIFACT_DIR}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n\n        RUNNER_TEST_RESULTS_DIR=\"${RUNNER_TEMP}/test-results\"\n        rm -rf \"${RUNNER_TEST_RESULTS_DIR}\"\n        mkdir -p \"${RUNNER_TEST_RESULTS_DIR}\"\n        echo \"RUNNER_TEST_RESULTS_DIR=${RUNNER_TEST_RESULTS_DIR}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n\n        RUNNER_DOCS_DIR=\"${RUNNER_TEMP}/docs\"\n        rm -rf \"${RUNNER_DOCS_DIR}\"\n        mkdir -p \"${RUNNER_DOCS_DIR}\"\n        echo \"RUNNER_DOCS_DIR=${RUNNER_DOCS_DIR}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n\n    - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        env | grep '^GITHUB' >> \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n        env | grep '^CI' >> \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n\n    - name: ROCm set GPU_FLAG\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        # All GPUs are visible to the runner; visibility, if needed, will be set by run_test.py.\n        # Add render group for container creation.\n        render_gid=`cat /etc/group | grep render | cut -d: -f3`\n        # Ensure GPU isolation if pod is part of kubernetes setup with DEVICE_FLAG.\n        if [ -f \"/etc/podinfo/gha-render-devices\" ]; then\n          DEVICE_FLAG=$(cat /etc/podinfo/gha-render-devices)\n        else\n          DEVICE_FLAG=\"--device /dev/dri\"\n        fi\n        # The --group-add daemon and --group-add bin are needed in the Ubuntu 24.04 and Almalinux OSs respectively.\n        # This is due to the device files (/dev/kfd & /dev/dri) being owned by video group on bare metal.\n        # This video group ID maps to subgid 1 inside the docker image due to the /etc/subgid entries.\n        # The group name corresponding to group ID 1 can change depending on the OS, so both are necessary.\n        echo \"GPU_FLAG=--device=/dev/mem --device=/dev/kfd $DEVICE_FLAG --group-add video --group-add $render_gid --group-add daemon --group-add bin --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --network=host\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n\n    - name: configure aws credentials\n      id: aws_creds\n      uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\n      with:\n        role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\n        aws-region: us-east-1\n        role-duration-seconds: 18000\n\n    - name: Login to Amazon ECR\n      id: login-ecr\n      continue-on-error: true\n      uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@062b18b96a7aff071d4dc91bc00c4c1a7945b076 # v2.0.1\n\n    - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        env | grep '^GITHUB' >> \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n        env | grep '^CI' >> \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n        env | grep '^RUNNER' >> \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/.github/actions/setup-rocm/action.yml",
    "numLines": 134,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 134
  },
  "type": "text"
}
194. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/actions/binary-docker-build/action.yml",
  "new_string": "      uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10",
  "old_string": "      uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/.github/actions/binary-docker-build/action.yml",
  "newString": "      uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10",
  "oldString": "      uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main",
  "originalFile": "name: Binary docker build\n\ndescription: Build docker image for binary builds\n\ninputs:\n  docker-image-name:\n    description: Docker image name for PR builds\n    required: true\n  docker-build-dir:\n    description: Location of the build.sh relative to .ci/docker\n    required: true\n  custom-tag-prefix:\n    description: Custom tag prefix for the docker image\n    required: false\n  DOCKER_TOKEN:\n    description: Docker token for authentication\n    required: true\n  DOCKER_ID:\n    description: Docker ID for authentication\n    required: true\n\nruns:\n  using: composite\n  steps:\n    - name: Checkout PyTorch\n      uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main\n\n    - name: Calculate docker image\n      id: calculate-docker-image\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main\n      with:\n        docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}\n        docker-build-dir: .ci/docker\n        custom-tag-prefix: ${{ inputs.custom-tag-prefix }}\n        docker-build-script: ${{ inputs.docker-build-dir }}/build.sh\n        always-rebuild: true\n        push: true\n\n    - name: Tag and (if WITH_PUSH) push docker image to docker.io\n      env:\n        DOCKER_TOKEN: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_TOKEN }}\n        DOCKER_ID: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_ID }}\n        DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}\n        DOCKER_IMAGE_PREFIX: ${{ inputs.custom-tag-prefix }}\n        CREATED_FULL_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        set -euox pipefail\n        GITHUB_REF=${GITHUB_REF:-$(git symbolic-ref -q HEAD || git describe --tags --exact-match)}\n        GIT_BRANCH_NAME=${GITHUB_REF##*/}\n        GIT_COMMIT_SHA=${GITHUB_SHA:-$(git rev-parse HEAD)}\n        CI_FOLDER_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD:.ci/docker)\n\n        DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX=docker.io/pytorch/${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME}:${DOCKER_IMAGE_PREFIX}\n\n        docker tag ${CREATED_FULL_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME} ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}\n        docker tag ${CREATED_FULL_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME} ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${GIT_BRANCH_NAME}\n        docker tag ${CREATED_FULL_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME} ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${GIT_COMMIT_SHA}\n        docker tag ${CREATED_FULL_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME} ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${CI_FOLDER_SHA}\n\n        # Pretty sure Github will mask tokens and I'm not sure if it will even be\n        # printed due to pipe, but just in case\n        set +x\n        if [[ ${WITH_PUSH:-false} == \"true\" ]]; then\n          echo \"${DOCKER_TOKEN}\" | docker login -u \"${DOCKER_ID}\" --password-stdin\n          docker push ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}\n          docker push ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${GIT_BRANCH_NAME}\n          docker push ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${GIT_COMMIT_SHA}\n          docker push ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${CI_FOLDER_SHA}\n        fi\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "   using: composite",
        "   steps:",
        "     - name: Checkout PyTorch",
        "-      uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main",
        "+      uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10",
        " ",
        "     - name: Calculate docker image",
        "       id: calculate-docker-image"
      ],
      "newLines": 7,
      "newStart": 23,
      "oldLines": 7,
      "oldStart": 23
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

{
  "filePath": "/workspace/.github/actions/binary-docker-build/action.yml",
  "newString": "      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10",
  "oldString": "      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main",
  "originalFile": "name: Binary docker build\n\ndescription: Build docker image for binary builds\n\ninputs:\n  docker-image-name:\n    description: Docker image name for PR builds\n    required: true\n  docker-build-dir:\n    description: Location of the build.sh relative to .ci/docker\n    required: true\n  custom-tag-prefix:\n    description: Custom tag prefix for the docker image\n    required: false\n  DOCKER_TOKEN:\n    description: Docker token for authentication\n    required: true\n  DOCKER_ID:\n    description: Docker ID for authentication\n    required: true\n\nruns:\n  using: composite\n  steps:\n    - name: Checkout PyTorch\n      uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n\n    - name: Calculate docker image\n      id: calculate-docker-image\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main\n      with:\n        docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}\n        docker-build-dir: .ci/docker\n        custom-tag-prefix: ${{ inputs.custom-tag-prefix }}\n        docker-build-script: ${{ inputs.docker-build-dir }}/build.sh\n        always-rebuild: true\n        push: true\n\n    - name: Tag and (if WITH_PUSH) push docker image to docker.io\n      env:\n        DOCKER_TOKEN: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_TOKEN }}\n        DOCKER_ID: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_ID }}\n        DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}\n        DOCKER_IMAGE_PREFIX: ${{ inputs.custom-tag-prefix }}\n        CREATED_FULL_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        set -euox pipefail\n        GITHUB_REF=${GITHUB_REF:-$(git symbolic-ref -q HEAD || git describe --tags --exact-match)}\n        GIT_BRANCH_NAME=${GITHUB_REF##*/}\n        GIT_COMMIT_SHA=${GITHUB_SHA:-$(git rev-parse HEAD)}\n        CI_FOLDER_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD:.ci/docker)\n\n        DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX=docker.io/pytorch/${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME}:${DOCKER_IMAGE_PREFIX}\n\n        docker tag ${CREATED_FULL_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME} ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}\n        docker tag ${CREATED_FULL_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME} ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${GIT_BRANCH_NAME}\n        docker tag ${CREATED_FULL_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME} ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${GIT_COMMIT_SHA}\n        docker tag ${CREATED_FULL_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME} ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${CI_FOLDER_SHA}\n\n        # Pretty sure Github will mask tokens and I'm not sure if it will even be\n        # printed due to pipe, but just in case\n        set +x\n        if [[ ${WITH_PUSH:-false} == \"true\" ]]; then\n          echo \"${DOCKER_TOKEN}\" | docker login -u \"${DOCKER_ID}\" --password-stdin\n          docker push ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}\n          docker push ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${GIT_BRANCH_NAME}\n          docker push ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${GIT_COMMIT_SHA}\n          docker push ${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${CI_FOLDER_SHA}\n        fi\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [

196. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/actions/linux-test/action.yml",
  "new_string": "      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10",
  "old_string": "      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/.github/actions/linux-test/action.yml",
  "newString": "      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10",
  "oldString": "      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main",
  "originalFile": "name: linux-test\n\ninputs:\n  build-environment:\n    required: true\n    type: string\n    description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\n  test-matrix:\n    required: true\n    type: string\n    description: JSON description of what test configs to run.\n  docker-image:\n    required: true\n    type: string\n    description: Docker image to run in.\n  sync-tag:\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"\"\n    description: |\n      If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\n      job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\n  use-gha:\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"\"\n    description: If set to any value, upload to GHA. Otherwise upload to S3.\n  dashboard-tag:\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"\"\n  s3-bucket:\n    description: S3 bucket to download artifact\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"gha-artifacts\"\n  aws-role-to-assume:\n    description: role to assume for downloading artifacts\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"\"\n  HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\n    description: |\n      HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\n    required: false\n    default: \"\"\n  GITHUB_TOKEN:\n    description: GitHub token\n    required: true\n  disable-monitor:\n    description: |\n      [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.\n      Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,\n      since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.\n    required: false\n    type: boolean\n    default: true\n#env:\n#  GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ inputs.default_branch }}\n\nruns:\n  using: composite\n  steps:\n    - name: Setup Linux\n      uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\n\n    - name: configure aws credentials\n      if : ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' }}\n      uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4\n      with:\n        role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}\n        role-session-name: gha-linux-test\n        aws-region: us-east-1\n\n    - name: Calculate docker image\n      id: calculate-docker-image\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main\n      with:\n        docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n\n    - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image\n      id: print-ghcr-mirror\n      env:\n        ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*/}\n        echo \"docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}\"\n\n    - name: Pull docker image\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main\n      with:\n        docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n\n    - name: Check if in a container runner\n      shell: bash\n      id: check_container_runner\n      run: echo \"IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER=$(if [ -f /.inarc ] || [ -f /.incontainer ]; then echo true ; else echo false; fi)\" >> \"$GITHUB_OUTPUT\"\n\n    - name: Install nvidia driver, nvidia-docker runtime, set GPU_FLAG\n      id: install-nvidia-driver\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@main\n      if: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'cuda') && !contains(matrix.config, 'nogpu') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false' }}\n\n    - name: Setup GPU_FLAG for docker run\n      id: setup-gpu-flag\n      run: echo \"GPU_FLAG=--gpus all -e NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES=all\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n      if: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'cuda') && !contains(matrix.config, 'nogpu') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' }}\n\n    - name: Setup SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT environment for docker run when on container\n      id: setup-sscache-port-flag\n      run: echo \"SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG=-e SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT=$((RUNNER_UID + 4226))\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n      if: ${{ steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' }}\n\n    - name: Lock NVIDIA A100 40GB Frequency\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        sudo nvidia-smi -pm 1\n        sudo nvidia-smi -ac 1215,1410\n        nvidia-smi\n      if: ${{ contains(matrix.runner, 'a100') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false' }}\n\n    - name: Start monitoring script\n      id: monitor-script\n      if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\n      shell: bash\n      continue-on-error: true\n      run: |\n        python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 nvidia-ml-py==11.525.84\n        python3 -m tools.stats.monitor > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &\n        echo \"monitor-script-pid=${!}\" >> \"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\"\n\n    - name: Download build artifacts\n      uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts\n      with:\n        name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n        s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\n\n    - name: Download TD artifacts\n      continue-on-error: true\n      uses: ./.github/actions/download-td-artifacts\n\n    - name: Parse ref\n      id: parse-ref\n      shell: bash\n      run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\n\n    - name: Get workflow job id\n      id: get-job-id\n      uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\n      if: always()\n      with:\n        github-token: ${{ inputs.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n    - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues\n      # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently\n      # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do\n      # any filtering.  All filtering is done in the build step.\n      id: keep-going\n      uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\n      with:\n        github-token: ${{ inputs.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\n        job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n\n    - name: Test\n      id: test\n      env:\n        BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n        PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n        GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\n        GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\n        GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\n        GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\n        GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\n        GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\n        JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n        JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n        BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\n        SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n        BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha || github.sha }}\n        TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}\n        SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}\n        NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}\n        REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.reenabled-issues }}\n        CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}\n        VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}\n        TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}\n        NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}\n        NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}\n        TD_DISTRIBUTED: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-td-distributed }}\n        SCCACHE_BUCKET: ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2\n        SCCACHE_REGION: us-east-1\n        SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX: ${{ github.workflow }}\n        SHM_SIZE: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'cuda') && '2g' || '1g' }}\n        DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n        XLA_CUDA: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'xla') && '0' || '' }}\n        XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla\n        PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}\n        PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}\n        DASHBOARD_TAG: ${{ inputs.dashboard-tag }}\n        HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ inputs.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}\n        SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}\n        IS_A100_RUNNER: ${{ contains(matrix.runner, 'a100') && '1' || '0' }}\n\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        set -x\n\n        if [[ $TEST_CONFIG == 'multigpu' ]]; then\n          TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/multigpu-test.sh\n        elif [[ $BUILD_ENVIRONMENT == *onnx* ]]; then\n          TEST_COMMAND=.ci/onnx/test.sh\n        else\n          TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test.sh\n        fi\n\n        # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\n        # TODO: Stop building test binaries as part of the build phase\n        # Used for GPU_FLAG since that doesn't play nice\n        # shellcheck disable=SC2086,SC2090\n        container_name=$(docker run \\\n          ${GPU_FLAG:-} \\\n          ${SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG:-} \\\n          -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\n          -e PR_NUMBER \\\n          -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \\\n          -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \\\n          -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \\\n          -e GITHUB_JOB \\\n          -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \\\n          -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \\\n          -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \\\n          -e JOB_ID \\\n          -e JOB_NAME \\\n          -e BASE_SHA \\\n          -e BRANCH \\\n          -e SHA1 \\\n          -e AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \\\n          -e IN_WHEEL_TEST \\\n          -e SHARD_NUMBER \\\n          -e TEST_CONFIG \\\n          -e NUM_TEST_SHARDS \\\n          -e REENABLED_ISSUES \\\n          -e CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR \\\n          -e VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS \\\n          -e NO_TEST_TIMEOUT \\\n          -e NO_TD \\\n          -e TD_DISTRIBUTED \\\n          -e PR_LABELS \\\n          -e MAX_JOBS=\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\" \\\n          -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \\\n          -e SCCACHE_REGION \\\n          -e SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX \\\n          -e XLA_CUDA \\\n          -e XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME \\\n          -e PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK \\\n          -e PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS \\\n          -e SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1 \\\n          -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \\\n          -e SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN \\\n          -e DASHBOARD_TAG \\\n          -e IS_A100_RUNNER \\\n          --env-file=\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\" \\\n          --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\n          --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\n          --ipc=host \\\n          --shm-size=\"${SHM_SIZE}\" \\\n          --tty \\\n          --detach \\\n          --name=\"${container_name}\" \\\n          --user jenkins \\\n          -v \"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\" \\\n          -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\n          \"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\"\n        )\n        echo \"DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID=${container_name}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n        docker exec -t \"${container_name}\" sh -c \"pip install $(echo dist/*.whl)[opt-einsum] && ${TEST_COMMAND}\"\n\n    - name: Upload pytest cache if tests failed\n      uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-upload\n      continue-on-error: true\n      if: failure() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion == 'failure'\n      with:\n        cache_dir: .pytest_cache\n        shard: ${{ matrix.shard }}\n        sha: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n        test_config: ${{ matrix.config }}\n        job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n\n    - name: Print remaining test logs\n      shell: bash\n      if: always() && steps.test.conclusion\n      run: |\n        cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true\n\n    - name: Stop monitoring script\n      if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n      shell: bash\n      continue-on-error: true\n      env:\n        MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n      run: |\n        kill \"$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID\"\n\n    - name: Upload test artifacts\n      uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts\n      if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'\n      with:\n        file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n        use-gha: ${{ inputs.use-gha }}\n        s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\n\n    - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        # shellcheck disable=SC2156\n        find . -iname \"core.[1-9]*\" -exec docker exec \"${DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID}\" sh -c \"gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'\" \\;\n\n    - name: Store Core dumps on S3\n      uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@v5\n      if: failure()\n      with:\n        name: coredumps-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}\n        retention-days: 14\n        if-no-files-found: ignore\n        path: ./**/core.[1-9]*\n\n    - name: Teardown Linux\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main\n      if: always() && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false'\n\n    # NB: We are currently having an intermittent GPU-related issue on G5 runners with\n    # A10G GPU. Once this happens, trying to reset the GPU as done in setup-nvidia does\n    # not seem to help. Here are some symptoms:\n    #   * Calling nvidia-smi timeouts after 60 second\n    #   * Fail to run nvidia-smi with an unable to determine the device handle for GPU\n    #     unknown error\n    #   * Test fails with a missing CUDA GPU error when initializing CUDA in PyTorch\n    #   * Run docker --gpus all fails with error response from daemon\n    #\n    # As both the root cause and recovery path are unclear, let's take the runner out of\n    # service so that it doesn't get any more jobs\n    - name: Check NVIDIA driver installation step\n      if: failure() && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outcome && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outcome != 'skipped'\n      shell: bash\n      env:\n        RUNNER_WORKSPACE: ${{ runner.workspace }}\n      run: |\n        set +e\n        set -x\n\n        nvidia-smi\n        # NB: Surprisingly, nvidia-smi command returns successfully with return code 0 even in\n        # the case where the driver has already crashed as it still can get the driver version\n        # and some basic information like the bus ID.  However, the rest of the information\n        # would be missing (ERR!), for example:\n        #\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        # | NVIDIA-SMI 525.89.02    Driver Version: 525.89.02    CUDA Version: 12.0     |\n        # |-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n        # | GPU  Name        Persistence-M| Bus-Id        Disp.A | Volatile Uncorr. ECC |\n        # | Fan  Temp  Perf  Pwr:Usage/Cap|         Memory-Usage | GPU-Util  Compute M. |\n        # |                               |                      |               MIG M. |\n        # |===============================+======================+======================|\n        # |   0  ERR!                Off  | 00000000:00:1E.0 Off |                 ERR! |\n        # |ERR!  ERR! ERR!    ERR! / ERR! |   4184MiB / 23028MiB |    ERR!      Default |\n        # |                               |                      |                 ERR! |\n        # +-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n        #\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        # | Processes:                                                                  |\n        # |  GPU   GI   CI        PID   Type   Process name                  GPU Memory |\n        # |        ID   ID                                                   Usage      |\n        # |=============================================================================|\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        #\n        # This should be reported as a failure instead as it will guarantee to fail when\n        # Docker tries to run with --gpus all\n        #\n        # So, the correct check here is to query one of the missing piece of info like\n        # GPU name, so that the command can fail accordingly\n        nvidia-smi --query-gpu=gpu_name --format=csv,noheader --id=0\n        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n\n        # For runner with multiple GPUs, we also want to confirm that the number of GPUs are the\n        # power of 2, i.e. 1, 2, 4, or 8. This is to avoid flaky test issue when one GPU fails\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/4000\n        GPU_COUNT=$(nvidia-smi --list-gpus | wc -l)\n        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n\n        # Check the GPU count to be a power of 2\n        if [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -le 8 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 1 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 2 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 4 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 8 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver detects $GPU_COUNT GPUs. The runner has a broken GPU, shutting it down...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " ",
        "     - name: Calculate docker image",
        "       id: calculate-docker-image",
        "-      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main",
        "+      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10",
        "       with:",
        "         docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}",
        " "
      ],
      "newLines": 7,
      "newStart": 74,
      "oldLines": 7,
      "oldStart": 74
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}
197. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/actions/linux-test/action.yml",
  "new_string": "      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10",
  "old_string": "      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/.github/actions/linux-test/action.yml",
  "newString": "      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10",
  "oldString": "      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main",
  "originalFile": "name: linux-test\n\ninputs:\n  build-environment:\n    required: true\n    type: string\n    description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\n  test-matrix:\n    required: true\n    type: string\n    description: JSON description of what test configs to run.\n  docker-image:\n    required: true\n    type: string\n    description: Docker image to run in.\n  sync-tag:\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"\"\n    description: |\n      If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\n      job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\n  use-gha:\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"\"\n    description: If set to any value, upload to GHA. Otherwise upload to S3.\n  dashboard-tag:\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"\"\n  s3-bucket:\n    description: S3 bucket to download artifact\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"gha-artifacts\"\n  aws-role-to-assume:\n    description: role to assume for downloading artifacts\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"\"\n  HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\n    description: |\n      HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\n    required: false\n    default: \"\"\n  GITHUB_TOKEN:\n    description: GitHub token\n    required: true\n  disable-monitor:\n    description: |\n      [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.\n      Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,\n      since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.\n    required: false\n    type: boolean\n    default: true\n#env:\n#  GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ inputs.default_branch }}\n\nruns:\n  using: composite\n  steps:\n    - name: Setup Linux\n      uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\n\n    - name: configure aws credentials\n      if : ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' }}\n      uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4\n      with:\n        role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}\n        role-session-name: gha-linux-test\n        aws-region: us-east-1\n\n    - name: Calculate docker image\n      id: calculate-docker-image\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\n      with:\n        docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n\n    - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image\n      id: print-ghcr-mirror\n      env:\n        ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*/}\n        echo \"docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}\"\n\n    - name: Pull docker image\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main\n      with:\n        docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n\n    - name: Check if in a container runner\n      shell: bash\n      id: check_container_runner\n      run: echo \"IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER=$(if [ -f /.inarc ] || [ -f /.incontainer ]; then echo true ; else echo false; fi)\" >> \"$GITHUB_OUTPUT\"\n\n    - name: Install nvidia driver, nvidia-docker runtime, set GPU_FLAG\n      id: install-nvidia-driver\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@main\n      if: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'cuda') && !contains(matrix.config, 'nogpu') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false' }}\n\n    - name: Setup GPU_FLAG for docker run\n      id: setup-gpu-flag\n      run: echo \"GPU_FLAG=--gpus all -e NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES=all\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n      if: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'cuda') && !contains(matrix.config, 'nogpu') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' }}\n\n    - name: Setup SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT environment for docker run when on container\n      id: setup-sscache-port-flag\n      run: echo \"SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG=-e SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT=$((RUNNER_UID + 4226))\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n      if: ${{ steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' }}\n\n    - name: Lock NVIDIA A100 40GB Frequency\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        sudo nvidia-smi -pm 1\n        sudo nvidia-smi -ac 1215,1410\n        nvidia-smi\n      if: ${{ contains(matrix.runner, 'a100') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false' }}\n\n    - name: Start monitoring script\n      id: monitor-script\n      if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\n      shell: bash\n      continue-on-error: true\n      run: |\n        python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 nvidia-ml-py==11.525.84\n        python3 -m tools.stats.monitor > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &\n        echo \"monitor-script-pid=${!}\" >> \"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\"\n\n    - name: Download build artifacts\n      uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts\n      with:\n        name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n        s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\n\n    - name: Download TD artifacts\n      continue-on-error: true\n      uses: ./.github/actions/download-td-artifacts\n\n    - name: Parse ref\n      id: parse-ref\n      shell: bash\n      run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\n\n    - name: Get workflow job id\n      id: get-job-id\n      uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\n      if: always()\n      with:\n        github-token: ${{ inputs.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n    - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues\n      # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently\n      # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do\n      # any filtering.
