All filtering is done in the build step.\n      id: keep-going\n      uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\n      with:\n        github-token: ${{ inputs.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\n        job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n\n    - name: Test\n      id: test\n      env:\n        BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n        PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n        GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\n        GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\n        GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\n        GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\n        GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\n        GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\n        JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n        JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n        BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\n        SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n        BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha || github.sha }}\n        TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}\n        SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}\n        NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}\n        REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.reenabled-issues }}\n        CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}\n        VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}\n        TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}\n        NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}\n        NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}\n        TD_DISTRIBUTED: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-td-distributed }}\n        SCCACHE_BUCKET: ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2\n        SCCACHE_REGION: us-east-1\n        SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX: ${{ github.workflow }}\n        SHM_SIZE: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'cuda') && '2g' || '1g' }}\n        DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n        XLA_CUDA: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'xla') && '0' || '' }}\n        XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla\n        PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}\n        PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}\n        DASHBOARD_TAG: ${{ inputs.dashboard-tag }}\n        HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ inputs.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}\n        SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}\n        IS_A100_RUNNER: ${{ contains(matrix.runner, 'a100') && '1' || '0' }}\n\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        set -x\n\n        if [[ $TEST_CONFIG == 'multigpu' ]]; then\n          TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/multigpu-test.sh\n        elif [[ $BUILD_ENVIRONMENT == *onnx* ]]; then\n          TEST_COMMAND=.ci/onnx/test.sh\n        else\n          TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test.sh\n        fi\n\n        # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\n        # TODO: Stop building test binaries as part of the build phase\n        # Used for GPU_FLAG since that doesn't play nice\n        # shellcheck disable=SC2086,SC2090\n        container_name=$(docker run \\\n          ${GPU_FLAG:-} \\\n          ${SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG:-} \\\n          -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\n          -e PR_NUMBER \\\n          -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \\\n          -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \\\n          -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \\\n          -e GITHUB_JOB \\\n          -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \\\n          -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \\\n          -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \\\n          -e JOB_ID \\\n          -e JOB_NAME \\\n          -e BASE_SHA \\\n          -e BRANCH \\\n          -e SHA1 \\\n          -e AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \\\n          -e IN_WHEEL_TEST \\\n          -e SHARD_NUMBER \\\n          -e TEST_CONFIG \\\n          -e NUM_TEST_SHARDS \\\n          -e REENABLED_ISSUES \\\n          -e CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR \\\n          -e VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS \\\n          -e NO_TEST_TIMEOUT \\\n          -e NO_TD \\\n          -e TD_DISTRIBUTED \\\n          -e PR_LABELS \\\n          -e MAX_JOBS=\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\" \\\n          -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \\\n          -e SCCACHE_REGION \\\n          -e SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX \\\n          -e XLA_CUDA \\\n          -e XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME \\\n          -e PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK \\\n          -e PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS \\\n          -e SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1 \\\n          -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \\\n          -e SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN \\\n          -e DASHBOARD_TAG \\\n          -e IS_A100_RUNNER \\\n          --env-file=\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\" \\\n          --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\n          --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\n          --ipc=host \\\n          --shm-size=\"${SHM_SIZE}\" \\\n          --tty \\\n          --detach \\\n          --name=\"${container_name}\" \\\n          --user jenkins \\\n          -v \"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\" \\\n          -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\n          \"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\"\n        )\n        echo \"DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID=${container_name}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n        docker exec -t \"${container_name}\" sh -c \"pip install $(echo dist/*.whl)[opt-einsum] && ${TEST_COMMAND}\"\n\n    - name: Upload pytest cache if tests failed\n      uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-upload\n      continue-on-error: true\n      if: failure() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion == 'failure'\n      with:\n        cache_dir: .pytest_cache\n        shard: ${{ matrix.shard }}\n        sha: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n        test_config: ${{ matrix.config }}\n        job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n\n    - name: Print remaining test logs\n      shell: bash\n      if: always() && steps.test.conclusion\n      run: |\n        cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true\n\n    - name: Stop monitoring script\n      if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n      shell: bash\n      continue-on-error: true\n      env:\n        MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n      run: |\n        kill \"$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID\"\n\n    - name: Upload test artifacts\n      uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts\n      if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'\n      with:\n        file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n        use-gha: ${{ inputs.use-gha }}\n        s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\n\n    - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        # shellcheck disable=SC2156\n        find . -iname \"core.[1-9]*\" -exec docker exec \"${DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID}\" sh -c \"gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'\" \\;\n\n    - name: Store Core dumps on S3\n      uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@v5\n      if: failure()\n      with:\n        name: coredumps-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}\n        retention-days: 14\n        if-no-files-found: ignore\n        path: ./**/core.[1-9]*\n\n    - name: Teardown Linux\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main\n      if: always() && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false'\n\n    # NB: We are currently having an intermittent GPU-related issue on G5 runners with\n    # A10G GPU. Once this happens, trying to reset the GPU as done in setup-nvidia does\n    # not seem to help. Here are some symptoms:\n    #   * Calling nvidia-smi timeouts after 60 second\n    #   * Fail to run nvidia-smi with an unable to determine the device handle for GPU\n    #     unknown error\n    #   * Test fails with a missing CUDA GPU error when initializing CUDA in PyTorch\n    #   * Run docker --gpus all fails with error response from daemon\n    #\n    # As both the root cause and recovery path are unclear, let's take the runner out of\n    # service so that it doesn't get any more jobs\n    - name: Check NVIDIA driver installation step\n      if: failure() && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outcome && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outcome != 'skipped'\n      shell: bash\n      env:\n        RUNNER_WORKSPACE: ${{ runner.workspace }}\n      run: |\n        set +e\n        set -x\n\n        nvidia-smi\n        # NB: Surprisingly, nvidia-smi command returns successfully with return code 0 even in\n        # the case where the driver has already crashed as it still can get the driver version\n        # and some basic information like the bus ID.  However, the rest of the information\n        # would be missing (ERR!), for example:\n        #\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        # | NVIDIA-SMI 525.89.02    Driver Version: 525.89.02    CUDA Version: 12.0     |\n        # |-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n        # | GPU  Name        Persistence-M| Bus-Id        Disp.A | Volatile Uncorr. ECC |\n        # | Fan  Temp  Perf  Pwr:Usage/Cap|         Memory-Usage | GPU-Util  Compute M. |\n        # |                               |                      |               MIG M. |\n        # |===============================+======================+======================|\n        # |   0  ERR!                Off  | 00000000:00:1E.0 Off |                 ERR! |\n        # |ERR!  ERR! ERR!    ERR! / ERR! |   4184MiB / 23028MiB |    ERR!      Default |\n        # |                               |                      |                 ERR! |\n        # +-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n        #\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        # | Processes:                                                                  |\n        # |  GPU   GI   CI        PID   Type   Process name                  GPU Memory |\n        # |        ID   ID                                                   Usage      |\n        # |=============================================================================|\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        #\n        # This should be reported as a failure instead as it will guarantee to fail when\n        # Docker tries to run with --gpus all\n        #\n        # So, the correct check here is to query one of the missing piece of info like\n        # GPU name, so that the command can fail accordingly\n        nvidia-smi --query-gpu=gpu_name --format=csv,noheader --id=0\n        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n\n        # For runner with multiple GPUs, we also want to confirm that the number of GPUs are the\n        # power of 2, i.e. 1, 2, 4, or 8. This is to avoid flaky test issue when one GPU fails\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/4000\n        GPU_COUNT=$(nvidia-smi --list-gpus | wc -l)\n        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n\n        # Check the GPU count to be a power of 2\n        if [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -le 8 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 1 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 2 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 4 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 8 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver detects $GPU_COUNT GPUs. The runner has a broken GPU, shutting it down...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "         echo \"docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}\"",
        " ",
        "     - name: Pull docker image",
        "-      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main",
        "+      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10",
        "       with:",
        "         docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}",
        " "
      ],
      "newLines": 7,
      "newStart": 88,
      "oldLines": 7,
      "oldStart": 88
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}
198. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/actions/linux-test/action.yml",
  "new_string": "      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@release/2.10",
  "old_string": "      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@main",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/.github/actions/linux-test/action.yml",
  "newString": "      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@release/2.10",
  "oldString": "      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@main",
  "originalFile": "name: linux-test\n\ninputs:\n  build-environment:\n    required: true\n    type: string\n    description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\n  test-matrix:\n    required: true\n    type: string\n    description: JSON description of what test configs to run.\n  docker-image:\n    required: true\n    type: string\n    description: Docker image to run in.\n  sync-tag:\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"\"\n    description: |\n      If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\n      job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\n  use-gha:\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"\"\n    description: If set to any value, upload to GHA. Otherwise upload to S3.\n  dashboard-tag:\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"\"\n  s3-bucket:\n    description: S3 bucket to download artifact\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"gha-artifacts\"\n  aws-role-to-assume:\n    description: role to assume for downloading artifacts\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"\"\n  HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\n    description: |\n      HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\n    required: false\n    default: \"\"\n  GITHUB_TOKEN:\n    description: GitHub token\n    required: true\n  disable-monitor:\n    description: |\n      [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.\n      Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,\n      since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.\n    required: false\n    type: boolean\n    default: true\n#env:\n#  GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ inputs.default_branch }}\n\nruns:\n  using: composite\n  steps:\n    - name: Setup Linux\n      uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\n\n    - name: configure aws credentials\n      if : ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' }}\n      uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4\n      with:\n        role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}\n        role-session-name: gha-linux-test\n        aws-region: us-east-1\n\n    - name: Calculate docker image\n      id: calculate-docker-image\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\n      with:\n        docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n\n    - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image\n      id: print-ghcr-mirror\n      env:\n        ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*/}\n        echo \"docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}\"\n\n    - name: Pull docker image\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n      with:\n        docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n\n    - name: Check if in a container runner\n      shell: bash\n      id: check_container_runner\n      run: echo \"IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER=$(if [ -f /.inarc ] || [ -f /.incontainer ]; then echo true ; else echo false; fi)\" >> \"$GITHUB_OUTPUT\"\n\n    - name: Install nvidia driver, nvidia-docker runtime, set GPU_FLAG\n      id: install-nvidia-driver\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@main\n      if: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'cuda') && !contains(matrix.config, 'nogpu') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false' }}\n\n    - name: Setup GPU_FLAG for docker run\n      id: setup-gpu-flag\n      run: echo \"GPU_FLAG=--gpus all -e NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES=all\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n      if: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'cuda') && !contains(matrix.config, 'nogpu') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' }}\n\n    - name: Setup SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT environment for docker run when on container\n      id: setup-sscache-port-flag\n      run: echo \"SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG=-e SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT=$((RUNNER_UID + 4226))\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n      if: ${{ steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' }}\n\n    - name: Lock NVIDIA A100 40GB Frequency\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        sudo nvidia-smi -pm 1\n        sudo nvidia-smi -ac 1215,1410\n        nvidia-smi\n      if: ${{ contains(matrix.runner, 'a100') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false' }}\n\n    - name: Start monitoring script\n      id: monitor-script\n      if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\n      shell: bash\n      continue-on-error: true\n      run: |\n        python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 nvidia-ml-py==11.525.84\n        python3 -m tools.stats.monitor > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &\n        echo \"monitor-script-pid=${!}\" >> \"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\"\n\n    - name: Download build artifacts\n      uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts\n      with:\n        name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n        s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\n\n    - name: Download TD artifacts\n      continue-on-error: true\n      uses: ./.github/actions/download-td-artifacts\n\n    - name: Parse ref\n      id: parse-ref\n      shell: bash\n      run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\n\n    - name: Get workflow job id\n      id: get-job-id\n      uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\n      if: always()\n      with:\n        github-token: ${{ inputs.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n    - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues\n      # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently\n      # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do\n      # any filtering.  All filtering is done in the build step.\n      id: keep-going\n      uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\n      with:\n        github-token: ${{ inputs.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\n        job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n\n    - name: Test\n      id: test\n      env:\n        BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n        PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n        GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\n        GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\n        GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\n        GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\n        GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\n        GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\n        JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n        JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n        BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\n        SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n        BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha || github.sha }}\n        TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}\n        SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}\n        NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}\n        REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.reenabled-issues }}\n        CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}\n        VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}\n        TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}\n        NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}\n        NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}\n        TD_DISTRIBUTED: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-td-distributed }}\n        SCCACHE_BUCKET: ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2\n        SCCACHE_REGION: us-east-1\n        SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX: ${{ github.workflow }}\n        SHM_SIZE: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'cuda') && '2g' || '1g' }}\n        DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n        XLA_CUDA: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'xla') && '0' || '' }}\n        XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla\n        PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}\n        PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}\n        DASHBOARD_TAG: ${{ inputs.dashboard-tag }}\n        HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ inputs.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}\n        SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}\n        IS_A100_RUNNER: ${{ contains(matrix.runner, 'a100') && '1' || '0' }}\n\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        set -x\n\n        if [[ $TEST_CONFIG == 'multigpu' ]]; then\n          TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/multigpu-test.sh\n        elif [[ $BUILD_ENVIRONMENT == *onnx* ]]; then\n          TEST_COMMAND=.ci/onnx/test.sh\n        else\n          TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test.sh\n        fi\n\n        # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\n        # TODO: Stop building test binaries as part of the build phase\n        # Used for GPU_FLAG since that doesn't play nice\n        # shellcheck disable=SC2086,SC2090\n        container_name=$(docker run \\\n          ${GPU_FLAG:-} \\\n          ${SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG:-} \\\n          -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\n          -e PR_NUMBER \\\n          -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \\\n          -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \\\n          -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \\\n          -e GITHUB_JOB \\\n          -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \\\n          -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \\\n          -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \\\n          -e JOB_ID \\\n          -e JOB_NAME \\\n          -e BASE_SHA \\\n          -e BRANCH \\\n          -e SHA1 \\\n          -e AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \\\n          -e IN_WHEEL_TEST \\\n          -e SHARD_NUMBER \\\n          -e TEST_CONFIG \\\n          -e NUM_TEST_SHARDS \\\n          -e REENABLED_ISSUES \\\n          -e CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR \\\n          -e VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS \\\n          -e NO_TEST_TIMEOUT \\\n          -e NO_TD \\\n          -e TD_DISTRIBUTED \\\n          -e PR_LABELS \\\n          -e MAX_JOBS=\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\" \\\n          -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \\\n          -e SCCACHE_REGION \\\n          -e SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX \\\n          -e XLA_CUDA \\\n          -e XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME \\\n          -e PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK \\\n          -e PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS \\\n          -e SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1 \\\n          -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \\\n          -e SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN \\\n          -e DASHBOARD_TAG \\\n          -e IS_A100_RUNNER \\\n          --env-file=\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\" \\\n          --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\n          --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\n          --ipc=host \\\n          --shm-size=\"${SHM_SIZE}\" \\\n          --tty \\\n          --detach \\\n          --name=\"${container_name}\" \\\n          --user jenkins \\\n          -v \"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\" \\\n          -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\n          \"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\"\n        )\n        echo \"DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID=${container_name}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n        docker exec -t \"${container_name}\" sh -c \"pip install $(echo dist/*.whl)[opt-einsum] && ${TEST_COMMAND}\"\n\n    - name: Upload pytest cache if tests failed\n      uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-upload\n      continue-on-error: true\n      if: failure() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion == 'failure'\n      with:\n        cache_dir: .pytest_cache\n        shard: ${{ matrix.shard }}\n        sha: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n        test_config: ${{ matrix.config }}\n        job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n\n    - name: Print remaining test logs\n      shell: bash\n      if: always() && steps.test.conclusion\n      run: |\n        cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true\n\n    - name: Stop monitoring script\n      if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n      shell: bash\n      continue-on-error: true\n      env:\n        MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n      run: |\n        kill \"$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID\"\n\n    - name: Upload test artifacts\n      uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts\n      if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'\n      with:\n        file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n        use-gha: ${{ inputs.use-gha }}\n        s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\n\n    - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        # shellcheck disable=SC2156\n        find . -iname \"core.[1-9]*\" -exec docker exec \"${DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID}\" sh -c \"gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'\" \\;\n\n    - name: Store Core dumps on S3\n      uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@v5\n      if: failure()\n      with:\n        name: coredumps-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}\n        retention-days: 14\n        if-no-files-found: ignore\n        path: ./**/core.[1-9]*\n\n    - name: Teardown Linux\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main\n      if: always() && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false'\n\n    # NB: We are currently having an intermittent GPU-related issue on G5 runners with\n    # A10G GPU. Once this happens, trying to reset the GPU as done in setup-nvidia does\n    # not seem to help. Here are some symptoms:\n    #   * Calling nvidia-smi timeouts after 60 second\n    #   * Fail to run nvidia-smi with an unable to determine the device handle for GPU\n    #     unknown error\n    #   * Test fails with a missing CUDA GPU error when initializing CUDA in PyTorch\n    #   * Run docker --gpus all fails with error response from daemon\n    #\n    # As both the root cause and recovery path are unclear, let's take the runner out of\n    # service so that it doesn't get any more jobs\n    - name: Check NVIDIA driver installation step\n      if: failure() && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outcome && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outcome != 'skipped'\n      shell: bash\n      env:\n        RUNNER_WORKSPACE: ${{ runner.workspace }}\n      run: |\n        set +e\n        set -x\n\n        nvidia-smi\n        # NB: Surprisingly, nvidia-smi command returns successfully with return code 0 even in\n        # the case where the driver has already crashed as it still can get the driver version\n        # and some basic information like the bus ID.  However, the rest of the information\n        # would be missing (ERR!), for example:\n        #\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        # | NVIDIA-SMI 525.89.02    Driver Version: 525.89.02    CUDA Version: 12.0     |\n        # |-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n        # | GPU  Name        Persistence-M| Bus-Id        Disp.A | Volatile Uncorr. ECC |\n        # | Fan  Temp  Perf  Pwr:Usage/Cap|         Memory-Usage | GPU-Util  Compute M. |\n        # |                               |                      |               MIG M. |\n        # |===============================+======================+======================|\n        # |   0  ERR!                Off  | 00000000:00:1E.0 Off |                 ERR! |\n        # |ERR!  ERR! ERR!    ERR! / ERR! |   4184MiB / 23028MiB |    ERR!      Default |\n        # |                               |                      |                 ERR! |\n        # +-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n        #\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        # | Processes:                                                                  |\n        # |  GPU   GI   CI        PID   Type   Process name                  GPU Memory |\n        # |        ID   ID                                                   Usage      |\n        # |=============================================================================|\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        #\n        # This should be reported as a failure instead as it will guarantee to fail when\n        # Docker tries to run with --gpus all\n        #\n        # So, the correct check here is to query one of the missing piece of info like\n        # GPU name, so that the command can fail accordingly\n        nvidia-smi --query-gpu=gpu_name --format=csv,noheader --id=0\n        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n\n        # For runner with multiple GPUs, we also want to confirm that the number of GPUs are the\n        # power of 2, i.e. 1, 2, 4, or 8. This is to avoid flaky test issue when one GPU fails\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/4000\n        GPU_COUNT=$(nvidia-smi --list-gpus | wc -l)\n        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n\n        # Check the GPU count to be a power of 2\n        if [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -le 8 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 1 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 2 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 4 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 8 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver detects $GPU_COUNT GPUs. The runner has a broken GPU, shutting it down...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " ",
        "     - name: Install nvidia driver, nvidia-docker runtime, set GPU_FLAG",
        "       id: install-nvidia-driver",
        "-      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@main",
        "+      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@release/2.10",
        "       if: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'cuda') && !contains(matrix.config, 'nogpu') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false' }}",
        " ",
        "     - name: Setup GPU_FLAG for docker run"
      ],
      "newLines": 7,
      "newStart": 99,
      "oldLines": 7,
      "oldStart": 99
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}
199. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/actions/linux-test/action.yml",
  "new_string": "      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10",
  "old_string": "      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/.github/actions/linux-test/action.yml",
  "newString": "      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10",
  "oldString": "      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main",
  "originalFile": "name: linux-test\n\ninputs:\n  build-environment:\n    required: true\n    type: string\n    description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\n  test-matrix:\n    required: true\n    type: string\n    description: JSON description of what test configs to run.\n  docker-image:\n    required: true\n    type: string\n    description: Docker image to run in.\n  sync-tag:\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"\"\n    description: |\n      If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\n      job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\n  use-gha:\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"\"\n    description: If set to any value, upload to GHA. Otherwise upload to S3.\n  dashboard-tag:\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"\"\n  s3-bucket:\n    description: S3 bucket to download artifact\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"gha-artifacts\"\n  aws-role-to-assume:\n    description: role to assume for downloading artifacts\n    required: false\n    type: string\n    default: \"\"\n  HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\n    description: |\n      HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\n    required: false\n    default: \"\"\n  GITHUB_TOKEN:\n    description: GitHub token\n    required: true\n  disable-monitor:\n    description: |\n      [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.\n      Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,\n      since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.\n    required: false\n    type: boolean\n    default: true\n#env:\n#  GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ inputs.default_branch }}\n\nruns:\n  using: composite\n  steps:\n    - name: Setup Linux\n      uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\n\n    - name: configure aws credentials\n      if : ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' }}\n      uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4\n      with:\n        role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}\n        role-session-name: gha-linux-test\n        aws-region: us-east-1\n\n    - name: Calculate docker image\n      id: calculate-docker-image\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\n      with:\n        docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n\n    - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image\n      id: print-ghcr-mirror\n      env:\n        ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*/}\n        echo \"docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}\"\n\n    - name: Pull docker image\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n      with:\n        docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n\n    - name: Check if in a container runner\n      shell: bash\n      id: check_container_runner\n      run: echo \"IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER=$(if [ -f /.inarc ] || [ -f /.incontainer ]; then echo true ; else echo false; fi)\" >> \"$GITHUB_OUTPUT\"\n\n    - name: Install nvidia driver, nvidia-docker runtime, set GPU_FLAG\n      id: install-nvidia-driver\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@release/2.10\n      if: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'cuda') && !contains(matrix.config, 'nogpu') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false' }}\n\n    - name: Setup GPU_FLAG for docker run\n      id: setup-gpu-flag\n      run: echo \"GPU_FLAG=--gpus all -e NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES=all\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n      if: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'cuda') && !contains(matrix.config, 'nogpu') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' }}\n\n    - name: Setup SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT environment for docker run when on container\n      id: setup-sscache-port-flag\n      run: echo \"SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG=-e SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT=$((RUNNER_UID + 4226))\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n      if: ${{ steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' }}\n\n    - name: Lock NVIDIA A100 40GB Frequency\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        sudo nvidia-smi -pm 1\n        sudo nvidia-smi -ac 1215,1410\n        nvidia-smi\n      if: ${{ contains(matrix.runner, 'a100') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false' }}\n\n    - name: Start monitoring script\n      id: monitor-script\n      if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\n      shell: bash\n      continue-on-error: true\n      run: |\n        python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 nvidia-ml-py==11.525.84\n        python3 -m tools.stats.monitor > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &\n        echo \"monitor-script-pid=${!}\" >> \"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\"\n\n    - name: Download build artifacts\n      uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts\n      with:\n        name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n        s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\n\n    - name: Download TD artifacts\n      continue-on-error: true\n      uses: ./.github/actions/download-td-artifacts\n\n    - name: Parse ref\n      id: parse-ref\n      shell: bash\n      run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\n\n    - name: Get workflow job id\n      id: get-job-id\n      uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\n      if: always()\n      with:\n        github-token: ${{ inputs.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n    - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues\n      # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently\n      # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do\n      # any filtering.  All filtering is done in the build step.\n      id: keep-going\n      uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\n      with:\n        github-token: ${{ inputs.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\n        job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n\n    - name: Test\n      id: test\n      env:\n        BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n        PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n        GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\n        GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\n        GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\n        GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\n        GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\n        GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\n        JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n        JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n        BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\n        SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n        BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha || github.sha }}\n        TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}\n        SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}\n        NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}\n        REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.reenabled-issues }}\n        CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}\n        VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}\n        TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}\n        NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}\n        NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}\n        TD_DISTRIBUTED: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-td-distributed }}\n        SCCACHE_BUCKET: ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2\n        SCCACHE_REGION: us-east-1\n        SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX: ${{ github.workflow }}\n        SHM_SIZE: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'cuda') && '2g' || '1g' }}\n        DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n        XLA_CUDA: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'xla') && '0' || '' }}\n        XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla\n        PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}\n        PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}\n        DASHBOARD_TAG: ${{ inputs.dashboard-tag }}\n        HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ inputs.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}\n        SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}\n        IS_A100_RUNNER: ${{ contains(matrix.runner, 'a100') && '1' || '0' }}\n\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        set -x\n\n        if [[ $TEST_CONFIG == 'multigpu' ]]; then\n          TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/multigpu-test.sh\n        elif [[ $BUILD_ENVIRONMENT == *onnx* ]]; then\n          TEST_COMMAND=.ci/onnx/test.sh\n        else\n          TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test.sh\n        fi\n\n        # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\n        # TODO: Stop building test binaries as part of the build phase\n        # Used for GPU_FLAG since that doesn't play nice\n        # shellcheck disable=SC2086,SC2090\n        container_name=$(docker run \\\n          ${GPU_FLAG:-} \\\n          ${SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG:-} \\\n          -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\n          -e PR_NUMBER \\\n          -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \\\n          -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \\\n          -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \\\n          -e GITHUB_JOB \\\n          -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \\\n          -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \\\n          -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \\\n          -e JOB_ID \\\n          -e JOB_NAME \\\n          -e BASE_SHA \\\n          -e BRANCH \\\n          -e SHA1 \\\n          -e AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \\\n          -e IN_WHEEL_TEST \\\n          -e SHARD_NUMBER \\\n          -e TEST_CONFIG \\\n          -e NUM_TEST_SHARDS \\\n          -e REENABLED_ISSUES \\\n          -e CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR \\\n          -e VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS \\\n          -e NO_TEST_TIMEOUT \\\n          -e NO_TD \\\n          -e TD_DISTRIBUTED \\\n          -e PR_LABELS \\\n          -e MAX_JOBS=\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\" \\\n          -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \\\n          -e SCCACHE_REGION \\\n          -e SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX \\\n          -e XLA_CUDA \\\n          -e XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME \\\n          -e PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK \\\n          -e PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS \\\n          -e SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1 \\\n          -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \\\n          -e SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN \\\n          -e DASHBOARD_TAG \\\n          -e IS_A100_RUNNER \\\n          --env-file=\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\" \\\n          --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\n          --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\n          --ipc=host \\\n          --shm-size=\"${SHM_SIZE}\" \\\n          --tty \\\n          --detach \\\n          --name=\"${container_name}\" \\\n          --user jenkins \\\n          -v \"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\" \\\n          -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\n          \"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\"\n        )\n        echo \"DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID=${container_name}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n        docker exec -t \"${container_name}\" sh -c \"pip install $(echo dist/*.whl)[opt-einsum] && ${TEST_COMMAND}\"\n\n    - name: Upload pytest cache if tests failed\n      uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-upload\n      continue-on-error: true\n      if: failure() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion == 'failure'\n      with:\n        cache_dir: .pytest_cache\n        shard: ${{ matrix.shard }}\n        sha: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n        test_config: ${{ matrix.config }}\n        job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n\n    - name: Print remaining test logs\n      shell: bash\n      if: always() && steps.test.conclusion\n      run: |\n        cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true\n\n    - name: Stop monitoring script\n      if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n      shell: bash\n      continue-on-error: true\n      env:\n        MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n      run: |\n        kill \"$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID\"\n\n    - name: Upload test artifacts\n      uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts\n      if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'\n      with:\n        file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n        use-gha: ${{ inputs.use-gha }}\n        s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\n\n    - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        # shellcheck disable=SC2156\n        find . -iname \"core.[1-9]*\" -exec docker exec \"${DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID}\" sh -c \"gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'\" \\;\n\n    - name: Store Core dumps on S3\n      uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@v5\n      if: failure()\n      with:\n        name: coredumps-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}\n        retention-days: 14\n        if-no-files-found: ignore\n        path: ./**/core.[1-9]*\n\n    - name: Teardown Linux\n      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main\n      if: always() && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false'\n\n    # NB: We are currently having an intermittent GPU-related issue on G5 runners with\n    # A10G GPU. Once this happens, trying to reset the GPU as done in setup-nvidia does\n    # not seem to help. Here are some symptoms:\n    #   * Calling nvidia-smi timeouts after 60 second\n    #   * Fail to run nvidia-smi with an unable to determine the device handle for GPU\n    #     unknown error\n    #   * Test fails with a missing CUDA GPU error when initializing CUDA in PyTorch\n    #   * Run docker --gpus all fails with error response from daemon\n    #\n    # As both the root cause and recovery path are unclear, let's take the runner out of\n    # service so that it doesn't get any more jobs\n    - name: Check NVIDIA driver installation step\n      if: failure() && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outcome && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outcome != 'skipped'\n      shell: bash\n      env:\n        RUNNER_WORKSPACE: ${{ runner.workspace }}\n      run: |\n        set +e\n        set -x\n\n        nvidia-smi\n        # NB: Surprisingly, nvidia-smi command returns successfully with return code 0 even in\n        # the case where the driver has already crashed as it still can get the driver version\n        # and some basic information like the bus ID.  However, the rest of the information\n        # would be missing (ERR!), for example:\n        #\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        # | NVIDIA-SMI 525.89.02    Driver Version: 525.89.02    CUDA Version: 12.0     |\n        # |-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n        # | GPU  Name        Persistence-M| Bus-Id        Disp.A | Volatile Uncorr. ECC |\n        # | Fan  Temp  Perf  Pwr:Usage/Cap|         Memory-Usage | GPU-Util  Compute M. |\n        # |                               |                      |               MIG M. |\n        # |===============================+======================+======================|\n        # |   0  ERR!                Off  | 00000000:00:1E.0 Off |                 ERR! |\n        # |ERR!  ERR! ERR!    ERR! / ERR! |   4184MiB / 23028MiB |    ERR!      Default |\n        # |                               |                      |                 ERR! |\n        # +-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n        #\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        # | Processes:                                                                  |\n        # |  GPU   GI   CI        PID   Type   Process name                  GPU Memory |\n        # |        ID   ID                                                   Usage      |\n        # |=============================================================================|\n        # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n        #\n        # This should be reported as a failure instead as it will guarantee to fail when\n        # Docker tries to run with --gpus all\n        #\n        # So, the correct check here is to query one of the missing piece of info like\n        # GPU name, so that the command can fail accordingly\n        nvidia-smi --query-gpu=gpu_name --format=csv,noheader --id=0\n        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n\n        # For runner with multiple GPUs, we also want to confirm that the number of GPUs are the\n        # power of 2, i.e. 1, 2, 4, or 8. This is to avoid flaky test issue when one GPU fails\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/4000\n        GPU_COUNT=$(nvidia-smi --list-gpus | wc -l)\n        NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n        # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n        if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n\n        # Check the GPU count to be a power of 2\n        if [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -le 8 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 1 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 2 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 4 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 8 ]; then\n          echo \"NVIDIA driver detects $GPU_COUNT GPUs. The runner has a broken GPU, shutting it down...\"\n          .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n        fi\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "         path: ./**/core.[1-9]*",
        " ",
        "     - name: Teardown Linux",
        "-      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main",
        "+      uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10",
        "       if: always() && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false'",
        " ",
        "     # NB: We are currently having an intermittent GPU-related issue on G5 runners with"
      ],
      "newLines": 7,
      "newStart": 328,
      "oldLines": 7,
      "oldStart": 328
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}
200. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/actions/setup-rocm/action.yml",
  "new_string": "      uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/diskspace-cleanup@release/2.10",
  "old_string": "      uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/diskspace-cleanup@main",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/.github/actions/setup-rocm/action.yml",
  "newString": "      uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/diskspace-cleanup@release/2.10",
  "oldString": "      uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/diskspace-cleanup@main",
  "originalFile": "name: Setup ROCm host\n\ndescription: Set up ROCm host for CI\n\nruns:\n  using: composite\n  steps:\n    - name: Runner ROCm version\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        dpkg -l | grep -E \"  rocm\"\n\n    - name: Stop all running docker containers\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        # ignore expansion of \"docker ps -q\" since it could be empty\n        # shellcheck disable=SC2046\n        docker stop $(docker ps -q) || true\n        # Prune all stopped containers.\n        docker container prune -f\n\n    - name: Runner health check system info\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        cat /etc/os-release || true\n        cat /etc/apt/sources.list.d/rocm.list || true\n        cat /opt/rocm/.info/version || true\n        whoami\n\n    - name: Runner health check amdgpu info\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        dpkg -l | grep -E \"  amdgpu\"\n\n    - name: Runner health check rocm-smi\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        rocm-smi\n\n    - name: Runner health check rocminfo\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        rocminfo\n\n    - name: Runner health check GPU count\n      if: always()\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        ngpu=$(rocminfo | grep -c -E 'Name:.*\\sgfx')\n        msg=\"Please file an issue on pytorch/pytorch reporting the faulty runner. Include a link to the runner logs so the runner can be identified\"\n        if [[ $ngpu -eq 0 ]]; then\n            echo \"Error: Failed to detect any GPUs on the runner\"\n            echo \"$msg\"\n            exit 1\n        fi\n\n    - name: Runner diskspace health check\n      uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/diskspace-cleanup@main\n      if: always()\n\n    - name: Runner health check disconnect on failure\n      if: ${{ failure() }}\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        killall runsvc.sh\n\n    - name: Setup useful environment variables\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        RUNNER_ARTIFACT_DIR=\"${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts\"\n        rm -rf \"${RUNNER_ARTIFACT_DIR}\"\n        mkdir -p \"${RUNNER_ARTIFACT_DIR}\"\n        echo \"RUNNER_ARTIFACT_DIR=${RUNNER_ARTIFACT_DIR}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n\n        RUNNER_TEST_RESULTS_DIR=\"${RUNNER_TEMP}/test-results\"\n        rm -rf \"${RUNNER_TEST_RESULTS_DIR}\"\n        mkdir -p \"${RUNNER_TEST_RESULTS_DIR}\"\n        echo \"RUNNER_TEST_RESULTS_DIR=${RUNNER_TEST_RESULTS_DIR}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n\n        RUNNER_DOCS_DIR=\"${RUNNER_TEMP}/docs\"\n        rm -rf \"${RUNNER_DOCS_DIR}\"\n        mkdir -p \"${RUNNER_DOCS_DIR}\"\n        echo \"RUNNER_DOCS_DIR=${RUNNER_DOCS_DIR}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n\n    - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        env | grep '^GITHUB' >> \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n        env | grep '^CI' >> \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n\n    - name: ROCm set GPU_FLAG\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        # All GPUs are visible to the runner; visibility, if needed, will be set by run_test.py.\n        # Add render group for container creation.\n        render_gid=`cat /etc/group | grep render | cut -d: -f3`\n        # Ensure GPU isolation if pod is part of kubernetes setup with DEVICE_FLAG.\n        if [ -f \"/etc/podinfo/gha-render-devices\" ]; then\n          DEVICE_FLAG=$(cat /etc/podinfo/gha-render-devices)\n        else\n          DEVICE_FLAG=\"--device /dev/dri\"\n        fi\n        # The --group-add daemon and --group-add bin are needed in the Ubuntu 24.04 and Almalinux OSs respectively.\n        # This is due to the device files (/dev/kfd & /dev/dri) being owned by video group on bare metal.\n        # This video group ID maps to subgid 1 inside the docker image due to the /etc/subgid entries.\n        # The group name corresponding to group ID 1 can change depending on the OS, so both are necessary.\n        echo \"GPU_FLAG=--device=/dev/mem --device=/dev/kfd $DEVICE_FLAG --group-add video --group-add $render_gid --group-add daemon --group-add bin --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --network=host\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n\n    - name: configure aws credentials\n      id: aws_creds\n      uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\n      with:\n        role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\n        aws-region: us-east-1\n        role-duration-seconds: 18000\n\n    - name: Login to Amazon ECR\n      id: login-ecr\n      continue-on-error: true\n      uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@062b18b96a7aff071d4dc91bc00c4c1a7945b076 # v2.0.1\n\n    - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker\n      shell: bash\n      run: |\n        env | grep '^GITHUB' >> \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n        env | grep '^CI' >> \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n        env | grep '^RUNNER' >> \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "         fi",
        " ",
        "     - name: Runner diskspace health check",
        "-      uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/diskspace-cleanup@main",
        "+      uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/diskspace-cleanup@release/2.10",
        "       if: always()",
        " ",
        "     - name: Runner health check disconnect on failure"
      ],
      "newLines": 7,
      "newStart": 61,
      "oldLines": 7,
      "oldStart": 61
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}
201. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/scripts/generate_binary_build_matrix.py",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\n\"\"\"Generates a matrix to be utilized through github actions\n\nWill output a condensed version of the matrix if on a pull request that only\nincludes the latest version of python we support built on three different\narchitectures:\n    * CPU\n    * Latest CUDA\n    * Latest ROCM\n    * Latest XPU\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nimport os\nimport re\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional\n\n\nSCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).absolute().parent\nREPO_ROOT = SCRIPT_DIR.parent.parent\n\n\nCUDA_ARCHES = [\"12.6\", \"12.8\", \"12.9\", \"13.0\"]\nCUDA_STABLE = \"12.8\"\nCUDA_ARCHES_FULL_VERSION = {\n    \"12.6\": \"12.6.3\",\n    \"12.8\": \"12.8.1\",\n    \"12.9\": \"12.9.1\",\n    \"13.0\": \"13.0.0\",\n}\nCUDA_ARCHES_CUDNN_VERSION = {\n    \"12.6\": \"9\",\n    \"12.8\": \"9\",\n    \"12.9\": \"9\",\n    \"13.0\": \"9\",\n}\n\nROCM_ARCHES = [\"7.0\", \"7.1\"]\n\nXPU_ARCHES = [\"xpu\"]\n\nCPU_AARCH64_ARCH = [\"cpu-aarch64\"]\n\nCPU_S390X_ARCH = [\"cpu-s390x\"]\n\nCUDA_AARCH64_ARCHES = [\"12.6-aarch64\", \"12.8-aarch64\", \"12.9-aarch64\", \"13.0-aarch64\"]\n\n\nPYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS = {\n    \"12.6\": (\n        \"cuda-bindings==12.9.4; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cuda-nvrtc-cu12==12.6.77; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cuda-runtime-cu12==12.6.77; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cuda-cupti-cu12==12.6.80; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cudnn-cu12==9.10.2.21; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cublas-cu12==12.6.4.1; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cufft-cu12==11.3.0.4; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-curand-cu12==10.3.7.77; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cusolver-cu12==11.7.1.2; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cusparse-cu12==12.5.4.2; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cusparselt-cu12==0.7.1; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-nccl-cu12==2.27.5; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-nvshmem-cu12==3.4.5; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-nvtx-cu12==12.6.77; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-nvjitlink-cu12==12.6.85; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cufile-cu12==1.11.1.6; platform_system == 'Linux'\"\n    ),\n    \"12.8\": (\n        \"cuda-bindings==12.9.4; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cuda-nvrtc-cu12==12.8.93; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cuda-runtime-cu12==12.8.90; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cuda-cupti-cu12==12.8.90; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cudnn-cu12==9.10.2.21; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cublas-cu12==12.8.4.1; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cufft-cu12==11.3.3.83; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-curand-cu12==10.3.9.90; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cusolver-cu12==11.7.3.90; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cusparse-cu12==12.5.8.93; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cusparselt-cu12==0.7.1; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-nccl-cu12==2.27.5; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-nvshmem-cu12==3.4.5; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-nvtx-cu12==12.8.90; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-nvjitlink-cu12==12.8.93; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cufile-cu12==1.13.1.3; platform_system == 'Linux'\"\n    ),\n    \"12.9\": (\n        \"cuda-bindings==12.9.4; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cuda-nvrtc-cu12==12.9.86; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cuda-runtime-cu12==12.9.79; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cuda-cupti-cu12==12.9.79; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cudnn-cu12==9.10.2.21; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cublas-cu12==12.9.1.4; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cufft-cu12==11.4.1.4; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-curand-cu12==10.3.10.19; get_nccl_wheel_version(arch_version: str) -> str:\n    requirements = map(\n        str.strip, re.split(\"[;|]\", PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS[arch_version])\n    )\n    return next(x for x in   "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\n\"\"\"Generates a matrix to be utilized through github actions\n\nWill output a condensed version of the matrix if on a pull request that only\nincludes the latest version of python we support built on three different\narchitectures:\n    * CPU\n    * Latest CUDA\n    * Latest ROCM\n    * Latest XPU\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nimport os\nimport re\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional\n\n\nSCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).absolute().parent\nREPO_ROOT = SCRIPT_DIR.parent.parent\n\n\nCUDA_ARCHES = [\"12.6\", \"12.8\", \"12.9\", \"13.0\"]\nCUDA_STABLE = \"12.8\"\nCUDA_ARCHES_FULL_VERSION = {\n    \"12.6\": \"12.6.3\",\n    \"12.8\": \"12.8.1\",\n    \"12.9\": \"12.9.1\",\n    \"13.0\": \"13.0.0\",\n}\nCUDA_ARCHES_CUDNN_VERSION = {\n    \"12.6\": \"9\",\n    \"12.8\": \"9\",\n    \"12.9\": \"9\",\n    \"13.0\": \"9\",\n}\n\nROCM_ARCHES = [\"7.0\", \"7.1\"]\n\nXPU_ARCHES = \"https://download.pytorch.org/whl/nightly\"\nNIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX = {\n    \"cpu\": dict(\n        name=\"cpu\",\n        index_url=f\"{PYTORCH_NIGHTLY_PIP_INDEX_URL}/cpu\",\n        supported_platforms=[\"Linux\", \"macOS\", \"Windows\"],\n        accelerator=\"cpu\",\n    )\n}\nCUDA_NIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX = {\n    f\"cuda-{major}.{minor}\": dict(\n        name=f\"cuda-{major}.{minor}\",\n        index_url=f\"{PYTORCH_NIGHTLY_PIP_INDEX_URL}/cu{major}{minor}\",\n        supported_platforms=[\"Linux\", \"Windows\"],\n        accelerator=\"cuda\",\n    )\n    for major, minor in (map(int, version.split(\".\")) for version in CUDA_ARCHES)\n}\nROCM_NIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX = {\n    f\"rocm-{major}.{minor}\": dict(\n        name=f\"rocm-{major}.{minor}\",\n        index_url=f\"{PYTORCH_NIGHTLY_PIP_INDEX_URL}/rocm{major}.{minor}\",\n        supported_platforms=[\"Linux\"],\n        accelerator=\"rocm\",\n    )\n    for major, minor in (map(int, version.split(\".\")) for version in ROCM_ARCHES)\n}\nXPU_NIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX = {\n    \"xpu\": dict(\n        name=\"xpu\",\n        index_url=f\"{PYTORCH_NIGHTLY_PIP_INDEX_URL}/xpu\",\n        supported_platforms=[\"Linux\"],\n        accelerator=\"xpu\",\n    )\n}\nNIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX.update(CUDA_NIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX)\nNIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX.update(ROCM_NIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX)\nNIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX.update(XPU_NIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX)\n\n\ndef get_nccl_wheel_version(arch_version: str) -> str:\n    requirements = map(\n        str.strip, re.split(\"[;|]\", PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS[arch_version])\n    )\n    return next(x for x in requirements if x.startswith(\"nvidia-nccl\")).split(\"==\")[1]\n\n\ndef read_nccl_pin(arch_version: str) -> str:\n    nccl_pin_path = (\n        REPO_ROOT\n        / \".ci\"\n        / \"docker\"\n        / \"ci_commit_pins\"\n        / f\"nccl-cu{arch_version[:2]}.txt\"\n    )\n    return nccl_pin_path.read_text().strip()\n\n\ndef validate_nccl_dep_consistency(arch_version: str) -> None:\n    nccl_release_tag = read_nccl_pin(arch_version)\n    wheel_ver = get_nccl_wheel_version(arch_version)\n    if not nccl_release_tag.startswith(f\"v{wheel_ver}\"):\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            f\"{arch_version} NCCL release tag version {nccl_release_tag} \"\n            f\"does not correspond to wheel version {wheel_ver}\"\n        )\n\n\ndef arch_type(arch_version: str) -> str:\n    if arch_version in CUDA_ARCHES:\n        return \"cuda\"\n    elif arch_version in ROCM_ARCHES:\n        return \"rocm\"\n    elif arch_version in XPU_ARCHES:\n        return \"xpu\"\n    elif arch_version in CPU_AARCH64_ARCH:\n        return \"cpu-aarch64\"\n    elif arch_version in CPU_S390X_ARCH:\n        return \"cpu-s390x\"\n    elif arch_version in CUDA_AARCH64_ARCHES:\n        return \"cuda-aarch64\"\n    else:  # arch_version should always be \"cpu\" in this case\n        return \"cpu\"\n\n\nDEFAULT_TAG = os.getenv(\"RELEASE_VERSION_TAG\", \"main\")\n\nWHEEL_CONTAINER_IMAGES = {\n    **{gpu_arch: f\"manylinux2_28-builder:cuda{gpu_arch}\" for gpu_arch in CUDA_ARCHES},\n    **{\n        gpu_arch: f\"manylinuxaarch64-builder:cuda{gpu_arch.replace('-aarch64', '')}\"\n        for gpu_arch in CUDA_AARCH64_ARCHES\n    },\n    **{gpu_arch: f\"manylinux2_28-builder:rocm{gpu_arch}\" for gpu_arch in ROCM_ARCHES},\n    \"xpu\": \"manylinux2_28-builder:xpu\",\n    \"cpu\": \"manylinux2_28-builder:cpu\",\n    \"cpu-aarch64\": \"manylinux2_28_aarch64-builder:cpu-aarch64\",\n    \"cpu-s390x\": \"pytorch/manylinuxs390x-builder:cpu-s390x\",\n}\n\nRELEASE = \"release\"\nDEBUG = \"debug\"\n\nLIBTORCH_CONTAINER_IMAGES: dict[str, str] = {\n    **{gpu_arch: f\"libtorch-cxx11-builder:cuda{gpu_arch}\" for gpu_arch in CUDA_ARCHES},\n    **{gpu_arch: f\"libtorch-cxx11-builder:rocm{gpu_arch}\" for gpu_arch in ROCM_ARCHES},\n    \"cpu\": \"libtorch-cxx11-builder:cpu\",\n}\n\nFULL_PYTHON_VERSIONS = [\"3.10\", \"3.11\", \"3.12\", \"3.13\", \"3.13t\", \"3.14\", \"3.14t\"]\n\n\ndef translate_desired_cuda(gpu_arch_type: str, gpu_arch_version: str) -> str:\n    return {\n        \"cpu\": \"cpu\",\n        \"cpu-aarch64\": \"cpu\",\n        \"cpu-s390x\": \"cpu\",\n        \"cuda\": f\"cu{gpu_arch_version.replace('.', '')}\",\n        \"cuda-aarch64\": f\"cu{gpu_arch_version.replace('-aarch64', '').replace('.', '')}\",\n        \"rocm\": f\"rocm{gpu_arch_version}\",\n        \"xpu\": \"xpu\",\n    }.get(gpu_arch_type, gpu_arch_version)\n\n\ndef list_without(in_list: list[str], without: list[str]) -> list[str]:\n    return [item for item in in_list if item not in without]\n\n\ndef generate_libtorch_matrix(\n    os: str,\n    release_type: str,\n    arches: Optional[list[str]] = None,\n    libtorch_variants: Optional[list[str]] = None,\n) -> list[dict[str, str]]:\n    if arches is None:\n        arches = [\"cpu\"]\n        if os == \"linux\":\n            arches += CUDA_ARCHES\n            arches += ROCM_ARCHES\n        elif os == \"windows\":\n            # TODO (huydhn): Only build CUDA 12.9 for Linux. This logic is to be cleaned up\n            # in 2.10\n            windows_cuda_arches = CUDA_ARCHES.copy()\n            windows_cuda_arches.remove(\"12.9\")\n            arches += windows_cuda_arches\n    if libtorch_variants is None:\n        libtorch_variants = [\n            \"shared-with-deps\",\n            \"shared-without-deps\",\n            \"static-with-deps\",\n            \"static-without-deps\",\n        ]\n\n    ret: list[dict[str, str]] = []\n    for arch_version in arches:\n        for libtorch_variant in libtorch_variants:\n            gpu_arch_type = arch_type(arch_version)\n            gpu_arch_version = \"\" if arch_version == \"cpu\" else arch_version\n            # ROCm builds without-deps failed even in ROCm runners; skip for now\n            if gpu_arch_type == \"rocm\" and (\"without-deps\" in libtorch_variant):\n                continue\n            ret.append(\n                {\n                    \"gpu_arch_type\": gpu_arch_type,\n                    \"gpu_arch_version\": gpu_arch_version,\n                    \"desired_cuda\": translate_desired_cuda(\n                        gpu_arch_type, gpu_arch_version\n                    ),\n                    \"libtorch_config\": release_type,\n                    \"libtorch_variant\": libtorch_variant,\n                    \"container_image\": (\n                        LIBTORCH_CONTAINER_IMAGES[arch_version].split(\":\")[0]\n                        if os not in (\"windows\", \"windows-arm64\")\n                        else \"\"\n                    ),\n                    \"container_image_tag_prefix\": (\n                        LIBTORCH_CONTAINER_IMAGES[arch_version].split(\":\")[1]\n                        if os not in (\"windows\", \"windows-arm64\")\n                        else \"\"\n                    ),\n                    \"package_type\": \"libtorch\",\n                    \"build_name\": f\"libtorch-{gpu_arch_type}{gpu_arch_version}-{libtorch_variant}-{release_type}\".replace(\n                        \".\", \"_\"\n                    ),\n                }\n            )\n    return ret\n\n\ndef generate_wheels_matrix(\n    os: str,\n    arches: Optional[list[str]] = None,\n    python_versions: Optional[list[str]] = None,\n) -> list[dict[str, str]]:\n    package_type = \"wheel\"\n    if os == \"linux\" or os == \"linux-aarch64\" or os == \"linux-s390x\":\n        # NOTE: We only build manywheel packages for x86_64 and aarch64 and s390x linux\n        package_type = \"manywheel\"\n\n    if python_versions is None:\n        python_versions = FULL_PYTHON_VERSIONS\n\n    if arches is None:\n        # Define default compute archivectures\n        arches = [\"cpu\"]\n        if os == \"linux\":\n            arches += CUDA_ARCHES + ROCM_ARCHES + XPU_ARCHES\n        elif os == \"windows\":\n            # TODO (huydhn): Only build CUDA 12.9 for Linux. This logic is to be cleaned up\n            # in 2.10\n            windows_cuda_arches = CUDA_ARCHES.copy()\n            windows_cuda_arches.remove(\"12.9\")\n            arches += windows_cuda_arches + XPU_ARCHES\n        elif os == \"linux-aarch64\":\n            # Separate new if as the CPU type is different and\n            # uses different build/test scripts\n            arches = CPU_AARCH64_ARCH + CUDA_AARCH64_ARCHES\n        elif os == \"linux-s390x\":\n            # Only want the one arch as the CPU type is different and\n            # uses different build/test scripts\n            arches = [\"cpu-s390x\"]\n\n    ret: list[dict[str, str]] = []\n    for python_version in python_versions:\n        for arch_version in arches:\n            gpu_arch_type = arch_type(arch_version)\n            gpu_arch_version = (\n                \"\"\n                if arch_version == \"cpu\"\n                or arch_version == \"cpu-aarch64\"\n                or arch_version == \"cpu-s390x\"\n                or arch_version == \"xpu\"\n                else arch_version\n            )\n\n            # TODO: Enable python 3.14 for rest\n            if os not in [\n                \"linux\",\n                \"linux-aarch64\",\n                \"linux-s390x\",\n                \"macos-arm64\",\n                \"windows\",\n            ] and (python_version == \"3.14\" or python_version == \"3.14t\"):\n                continue\n\n            # cuda linux wheels require PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS to install\n\n            if (\n                arch_version in [\"13.0\", \"12.9\", \"12.8\", \"12.6\"]\n                and os == \"linux\"\n                or arch_version in CUDA_AARCH64_ARCHES\n            ):\n                desired_cuda = translate_desired_cuda(gpu_arch_type, gpu_arch_version)\n                ret.append(\n                    {\n                        \"python_version\": python_version,\n                        \"gpu_arch_type\": gpu_arch_type,\n                        \"gpu_arch_version\": gpu_arch_version,\n                        \"desired_cuda\": desired_cuda,\n                        \"container_image\": WHEEL_CONTAINER_IMAGES[arch_version].split(\n                            \":\"\n                        )[0],\n                        \"container_image_tag_prefix\": WHEEL_CONTAINER_IMAGES[\n                            arch_version\n                        ].split(\":\")[1],\n                        \"package_type\": package_type,\n                        \"pytorch_extra_install_requirements\": (\n                            PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS[\n                                f\"{desired_cuda[2:4]}.{desired_cuda[4:]}\"  # for cuda-aarch64: cu126 -> 12.6\n                            ]\n                            if os == \"linux-aarch64\"\n                            else PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS[arch_version]\n                        ),\n                        \"build_name\": (\n                            f\"{package_type}-py{python_version}-{gpu_arch_type}\"\n                            f\"{'-' if 'aarch64' in gpu_arch_type else ''}{gpu_arch_version.replace('-aarch64', '')}\".replace(\n                                \".\", \"_\"\n                            )\n                        ),  # include special case for aarch64 build, remove the -aarch64 postfix\n                    }\n                )\n            else:\n                ret.append(\n                    {\n                        \"python_version\": python_version,\n                        \"gpu_arch_type\": gpu_arch_type,\n                        \"gpu_arch_version\": gpu_arch_version,\n                        \"desired_cuda\": translate_desired_cuda(\n                            gpu_arch_type, gpu_arch_version\n                        ),\n                        \"container_image\": WHEEL_CONTAINER_IMAGES[arch_version].split(\n                            \":\"\n                        )[0],\n                        \"container_image_tag_prefix\": WHEEL_CONTAINER_IMAGES[\n                            arch_version\n                        ].split(\":\")[1],\n                        \"package_type\": package_type,\n                        \"build_name\": f\"{package_type}-py{python_version}-{gpu_arch_type}{gpu_arch_version}\".replace(\n                            \".\", \"_\"\n                        ),\n                        \"pytorch_extra_install_requirements\": (\n                            PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS[\"xpu\"]\n                            if gpu_arch_type == \"xpu\"\n                            else \"\"\n                        ),\n                    }\n                )\n\n    return ret\n\n\narch_version = \"\"\nfor arch_version in CUDA_ARCHES:\n    validate_nccl_dep_consistency(arch_version)\ndel arch_version\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    # Used by tools/nightly.py\n    (SCRIPT_DIR / \"nightly_source_matrix.json\").write_text(\n        json.dumps(NIGHTLY_SOURCE_MATRIX, indent=4) + \"\\n\"\n    )\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " REPO_ROOT = SCRIPT_DIR.parent.parent",

        " ",
        "-CUDA_ARCHES = [\"12.6\", \"12.8\", \"12.9\", \"13.0\"]",
        "+CUDA_ARCHES = [\"12.6\", \"12.8\", \"13.0\"]",
        " CUDA_STABLE = \"12.8\"",
        " CUDA_ARCHES_FULL_VERSION = {",
        "     \"12.6\": \"12.6.3\","
      ],
      "newLines": 7,

}

  "oldString": "CUDA_ARCHES_FULL_VERSION = {\n    \"12.6\": \"12.6.3\",\n    \"12.8\": \"12.8.1\",\n    \"12.9\": \"12.9.1\",\n    \"13.0\": \"13.0.0\",\n}",
  "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\n\"\"\"Generates a matrix to be utilized through github actions\n\nWill output a condensed version of the matrix if on a pull request that only\nincludes the latest version of python we support built on three different\narchitectures:\n    * CPU\n    * Latest CUDA\n    * Latest ROCM\n    * Latest XPU\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nimport os\nimport re\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional\n\n\nSCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).absolute().parent\nREPO_ROOT = SCRIPT_DIR.parent.parent\n\n\nCUDA_ARCHES = [\"12.6\", \"12.8\", \"13.0\"]\nCUDA_STABLE = \"12.8\"\nCUDA_ARCHES_FULL_VERSION = {\n    \"12.6\": \"12.6.3\",\n    \"12.8\": \"12.8.1\",\n    \"12.9\": \"12.9.1\",\n    \"13.0\": \"13.0.0\",\n}\nCUDA_ARCHES_CUDNN_VERSION = {\n    \"12.6\": \"9\",\n    \"12.8\": \"9\",\n    \"12.9\": \"9\",\n    \"13.0\": \"9\",\n}\n\nROCM_ARCHES = [\"7.0\", \"7.1\"]\n\nXPU_ARCHES = [\"xpu\"]\n\nCPU_AARCH64_ARCH = [\"cpu-aarch64\"]\n\nCPU_S390X_ARCH = [\"cpu-s390x\"]\n\nCUDA_AARCH64_ARCHES = [\"12.6-aarch64\", \"12.8-aarch64\", \"12.9-aarch64\", \"13.0-aarch64\"]\n\n\nPYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS = {\n    \"12.6\": (\n platform_system == 'Linux'\"\n    ),\n    \"12.8\": (\n        \"cuda-bindings==12.9.4; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n        \"nvidia-cuda-nvrtc-cu12==12.8.93; platform_system == 'Linux' | \"\n
